title: Facebook Ads Specialist (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
We’re Hiring:
 Ads Specialist (Full-Time, Remote)



We are a fast growing marketing agency helping gyms and fitness studios worldwide generate consistent leads through
 and Instagram ads.



We are looking for a
 Ads Specialist with at least 2 years of hands-on experience managing Meta ad campaigns. This is not an entry level role. We are looking for someone confident in executing campaigns, understands performance data, and cares about getting results.



What You’ll Be Doing

• Building and managing
 and Instagram ad campaigns from start to finish

• Optimising campaigns based on performance data and insights

You Must Have

 and Instagram) ads

title: Customer Success Representative
description:
MISSION STATEMENT



SecureSpace Self Storage is one of the fastest-growing Self Storage platforms in the USA, with assets located primarily in high-density, urban markets. With a focus on exceptional customer service and upgraded security features, SecureSpace provides a high-quality experience that our customers can count on in any store they visit.



SCOPE OF POSITION



The Customer Success Representative at SecureSpace plays a crucial role in building strong relationships with customers by identifying their storage needs and offering tailored solutions using SecureSpace’s proprietary technology. This position involves actively engaging with customers to understand their requirements and challenges while leveraging cutting-edge technology to enhance customer experiences across all facility locations nationwide. The representative will perform all responsibilities from home, allowing flexibility while staying connected with customers and the team. This role thrives in a fast-paced and diverse environment, requiring a proactive and motivated individual excited about contributing to customer satisfaction and success in a technology-driven landscape.



SKILLS & EXPERIENCE:



· 2-5 years of customer service experience



· High school diploma or equivalent; college education is a plus.



· Excellent communication skills, approachable and responsive to customer needs.



· Demonstrates a proactive attitude, possessing a strong work ethic.



· Friendly and focused on service, able to anticipate and meet customer’s needs.



· Innovative and proactive problem solvers who adapt quickly to challenges and embrace learning opportunities.



· Self-motivated, independent workers who take initiative and are open to learning, always seeking opportunities for growth and improvement.



RESPONSIBILITIES: include (but are not limited to):



· Achieve and exceed sales targets by actively engaging with prospective customers through unit rentals, protection plans, and the sale of moving supplies.



· Foster and strengthen relationships with existing customers by providing exceptional service, managing payments, collecting late fees, and following up on outstanding balances.



· Assist new customers throughout the rental process and ensure they understand the agreements.



· Function independently daily while promoting teamwork and collaboration with other locations in a remote environment.



· Demonstrate strong consulting, troubleshooting, and adaptability skills to effectively resolve issues as a Customer Success Representative.



· Complete additional duties as assigned.



REPORTS TO: Customer Success Manager



PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS:



Qualified individuals must be able to perform the position's essential duties with or without accommodation. A qualified person with a disability may request a modification or adjustment to the job or work environment to meet the physical requirements of the position. GSA/SecureSpace will attempt to satisfy requests if the accommodations needed are reasonable and no undue hardship would result.



· Ability to perform essential job functions while seated for extended periods, including working on a computer and taking phone calls, as well as sit, walk, stand, bend, squat, climb, kneel, and twist intermittently or continuously.



· Capability to read documents and focus on computer screens, with or without corrective lenses as may be required.



· Ability to arrange the workspace efficiently for productivity, accommodating any individual needs for ergonomic considerations.



· Willingness to transition between various tasks and responsibilities while maintaining work efficiency and effectiveness.

title: Level Up, Engr! Turn Your EE/ECE/ME Degree into a Global Trade Career with Korea
description:
[Recruitment Validity Period]

Applications are open until 3 business days from the date of the first recruitment announcement.



[Join DongYang Engineering and Build Global Business Together with Filipino Talent]

- DongYang Engineering (DYE) is an engineering-based global trading company located in Ulsan, Korea, with approximately 30 employees.

- DYE provides engineering and procurement services in the shipbuilding, offshore, plant, and wind power industries. We also support sourcing and quotation work for electrical, electronic, and mechanical products by connecting overseas buyers with domestic Korean suppliers.

- In addition, DYE works with global partners such as Schneider Electric and Emerson, supplying our partners’ materials and services to customers in Korea.

- Currently, DYE has a 3-member overseas sales team composed of Korean and Nepalese tea
mbers who are fluent in English. We provide a supportive working environment so that new employees can adapt quickly.

- DYE also plans to expand its overseas sales and global operations team with a focus on Filipino talent. Therefore, this position offers future opportunities to grow into a team leader or management role.



[About the Position]

- We are looking for an Electrical & Electronics Inquiry / Quotation ERP Data Assistant.(Entry~Experienced Level)

- This position is responsible for checking overseas buyer inquiries and supplier quotation information, then accurately entering and managing the data in our company’s ERP program.

- Since the products are related to electrical, electronic, and mechanical fields, the candidate should be able to understand and distinguish basic product names, model numbers, specifications, quantities, unit prices, and quotation terms.

- The ERP input method can be learned by referring to our existing work records accumulated over the past 4 years. Internal tea
mbers will also provide guidance when needed.



[Company Introduction Materials]

- Company Introduction Video:

youtu.be/zCExthh9pzc?si=EkDCs7pqN6WylsWP

- Company
 Page:

www.
/dongyangeng



[Responsibilities]

- Check overseas buyer inquiries registered in the ERP program

- Review product information related to electrical, electronic, and mechanical products

- Organize product names, model numbers, specifications, quantities, unit prices, supplier information, and quotation terms

- Enter organized information into each required field in the ERP system

- Check and reflect quotation materials received from domestic Korean suppliers

- Refer to existing ERP input records and enter data according to the company’s internal standards

- Confirm unclear product information or specifications with internal tea
mbers

- Manage quotation progress and related data in an organized manner



[Required Qualifications & Skills]

This position requires basic technical understanding because it handles information related to electrical, electronic, and mechanical products.

- Degree or background in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, or a related technical field

- Basic understanding of electrical, electronic, or mechanical products

- Ability to distinguish product names, model numbers, specifications, quantities, unit prices, and quotation details

- Ability to carefully check documents, numbers, and technical information

- Basic Microsoft Excel and Word skills

- Ability to read English product inquiries and quotation documents and understand the basic contents

- Willingness to learn ERP programs and internal work systems

- Responsible attitude toward repetitive but important data entry tasks



[Preferred Experience]

The following experience is not mandatory, but it will be helpful for adapting to the role.

- Experience handling quotations, purchase orders, invoices, or product lists

- Experience using ERP, inventory management, purchasing management, or quotation management systems

- Experience in trading, export/import, purchasing, sourcing, or overseas sales support

- Experience communicating with suppliers or organizing supplier quotation data

- Experience reviewing product catalogs, spec sheets, manuals, or technical documents

- Experience with electrical/electronic components, industrial products, or machinery

- Experience organizing data using Excel



[Who We Are Looking For]

For this position, accurate understanding and careful organization are more important than fast typing.

This role is suitable for someone who:

- Handles documents and numbers accurately

- Can follow fixed formats and internal standards

- Does not guess unclear information and asks for confirmation when needed

- Can refer to previous examples and apply the same standards consistently

- Can handle repetitive data entry tasks responsibly

- Has an interest in global trade and technical products

- Wants to grow with a Korea-based global business team

ERP input data may be used for quotation review, supplier communication, and future export decisions, so a responsible work attitude is very important.



[Training & Work Support]

New employees are not expected to handle everything alone from the beginning.

DYE provides the following training and support:

- Existing ERP input records accumulated over the past 4 years

- Educational tutorial videos to help understand the work

- A mentorship program to help new employees adapt quickly

- Guidance from internal tea
mbers

- Additional explanations from a tea
mber who understands the current workflow



[Compensation & Employment Terms]

- This position is open to both entry-level or junior candidates and experienced candidates.

- Candidates with little or no related work experience will receive structured training under a designated mentor to adapt to our internal systems and operational standards.

- Candidates with 3 or more years of practical experience in mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, electronics engineering, trading, sourcing, quotation management, ERP data management, purchasing, export/import, or related fields may apply as experienced candidates.



[Payment Term: Monthly basis]

- Probationary Period: 6 months

- For entry-level candidates or candidates with limited related experience, the probationary salary will follow the company’s internal guideline below.

- Months 0–3: USD 280/month

- Months 4–6: USD 300/month

Subject to satisfactory performance evaluation after the first 3 months.

The probation period may be shortened if the employee demonstrates fast work adaptation and receives an excellent evaluation. This has occurred in multiple past cases.



- Candidates with 3 or more years of relevant practical experience should indicate their expected salary range when applying.

- This information will be included as a required field in the Google Form submitted during the first application stage.

When completing the Google Form, please provide:

* Minimum expected monthly salary

* Maximum expected monthly salary

* Brief summary of relevant experience supporting the requested salary range

* There will be a 6-month probationary period during which 80% of the contracted salary will be paid. However, the probationary period may be shortened depending on work performance.

* The final salary will be determined after reviewing the candidate’s experience, technical understanding, work capability, communication skills, and overall fit for the position.



[After Regularization]

- Annual contract renewal is standard after regularization.

- Salary adjustments may be made based on performance, teamwork, and overall evaluation.

- If overall performance, including work capability, teamwork, reliability, and internal evaluations, is considered outstanding, - salary increases may be granted within a shorter period. This has occurred in multiple past cases.

- Exceptional performance may also lead to early promotions or salary increases prior to the annual review.



[Perks & Benefits]

1. Regular Employee Benefits

- 13th Month Salary provided

- 10 additional paid leave days upon regularization

2. Performance-Based Incentives

- Additional incentives may be provided during high-performance business periods or when performance is excellent.

- Incentives are reviewed based on work attitude, responsibility, consistency, and overall contribution.

3. Paid Holidays

- January 1st, New Year’s Day, and Christmas Day are paid holidays.

- This applies to both probationary and regular employees.

4. Long Service Incentive Program

- 1 Year Service: PHP 17,000

- 3 Year Service: PHP 22,500

- 5 Year Service: PHP 28,000



[How To Apply for the job]

Just Fill out 10 Simple Questions through Google Form






Interview via google meet will be conducted after first screening and candidates will be notifed to their
s or
 



[Closing Message]

This position plays an important role in accurately organizing overseas buyer inquiries and domestic supplier quotations for electrical, electronic, and mechanical products in the ERP system.



If you have a background in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, or a related field, and you can handle documents and data carefully, you can successfully adapt to this role with DYE’s training and support.



As DYE plans to expand its global team with a focus on Filipino talent, this position offers the opportunity to grow into a larger role in the future.

title: Ad Creative Strategist (AI Video + Statics)
description:
Vera Health is a fast-growing health tech startup backed by Y Combinator. We've built a clinical decision support search engine used by doctors, nurses, pharmacists, and medical students worldwide. We're scaling aggressively and need a creative engine to fuel our paid growth on Meta and Google.



We are looking for an Ad Creative Strategist who doesn't wait to be told what to make. This is not an execution role. We need someone who generates ideas independently, produces high volumes of ad creatives, tests angles constantly, and learns fast from what works and what doesn't.



What You'll Be Doing



- Producing a steady volume of ad creatives every week — static images and AI-generated videos — without being micromanaged

- Independently coming up with new angles, hooks, and creative concepts to test

- Using AI video tools (Runway, Kling, Pika, CapCut, or similar) to produce video ads efficiently

- Designing scroll-stopping static creatives using Canva, Adobe Express, or similar tools

- Reviewing performance data with the team and spinning up new iterations based on what's working

- Thinking like a performance marketer: what makes someone stop scrolling, click, and convert



You Must Have



- A portfolio of ad creatives specifically — not just social media or brand content

- Hands-on experience with AI video generation tools (you use them regularly, not learning them)

- An understanding of direct response advertising: hooks, pattern interrupts, calls to action

- Strong visual instincts combined with a testing mindset

- The ability to produce at volume without sacrificing quality

- Strong written English for communicating ideas and creative rationale



You Might Be a Great Fit If You



- Get excited about launching 10 creative concepts and iterating on the winners

- Think about why an ad works or fails, not just whether it looks good

- Take full ownership of the creative pipeline without needing a detailed brief every time

- Stay organized and fast-moving even when juggling multiple creative formats

- Are genuinely curious about what resonates with different audiences



This Is Probably Not the Right Role If You



- Need a detailed brief before starting any creative work

- Focus more on aesthetics than on performance

- Have only produced organic social content with no paid ads experience



Why You'll Love Working With Us



- 100% remote — work from anywhere

- Weekends off

- Flexible hours as long as output and deadlines are met

- Be part of a mission-driven company making a real impact in healthcare

- Fast-growing startup with YC backing — real career trajectory here

- Work directly with the growth lead, no bureaucracy



Important



- Full-time role only (40 hours/week)

- No freelance work or additional jobs on the side

- Must have a reliable internet connection and your own equipment



To Apply, please send the following:



- Your portfolio or examples of ad creatives you've made (Meta or Google preferred)

- A 2-minute video recording/loom starting with: "My name is [Name], I'm [age] years old, and here's a recent ad creative I made and what I was trying to achieve with it." Then walk us through your process and why you'd be great at this role.



We're excited to find our creative partner. Looking forward to hearing from you.

title: Remote Customer Service Representative
description:

This role is ideal for someone who is confident managing operations behind the scenes, comfortable communicating with customers and tea
mbers, and capable of keeping things organized in a fast-paced environment. 



This is a long-term opportunity for someone who takes ownership, communicates well, and enjoys building structure, improving systems, and supporting daily business operations. 



What You’ll Be Doing:

-Handle inbound and outbound customer communication via phone,
, and SMS

-Manage scheduling, appointment coordination, and calendar updates

What I’m Looking For:

-Excellent written and verbal English communication

-Strong organizational and time management skills

-High attention to detail and strong follow-through

-Tech-savvy and quick to learn new tools and systems

-Administrative support

-Customer service (phone,
, SMS)

-Microsoft Office

-Canva

-ChatGPT / Claude

This Role Is a Great Fit If You:

-Like creating structure and keeping things organized

-Enjoy helping a business run smoothly behind the scenes

-Are proactive and take initiative without being micromanaged

-Want a long-term role where you can grow and take on more responsibility over time

title: Experienced B2B Cold Caller (Appointment Setter) For Huge Commission Role - $150
description:
* PLEASE READ THE ENTIRE DESCRIPTION BEFORE APPLYING *



Hey, I am JT! I am looking for 1-2 hard working appointment setters for my small business. I'm not a big call center where you are just a number. I'm a small business owner and looking for help doing outbound B2B cold calling for me.



I help small businesses with an A.I. Receptionist to answer their missed calls and after-hours calls. This is a simple, in-demand offer. I just need help introducing it and getting business owners interested.



YOUR ROLE:

-- Source your own leads, leads are not provided.

-- Call 60-80 new contacts per 4-hour shift.

-- Offer them a free consultation to learn about A.I.

-- If they show interest, schedule them for a consultation with me

-- That's it! Once they show-up to their appointment, I take over.



REQUIREMENTS (DO NOT APPLY UNLESS YOU MEET ALL THE CRITERIA):

-- Experienced B2B Cold Calling and Appointment Setting. This means 100% cold calling — no prior contact, no warm leads.

-- If you have only worked inbound leads, warm leads, or marketing leads, DO NOT APPLY.

-- Clear and confident English communication with no heavy accent.

-- You are someone who is money hungry and motivated to hit your targets.

-- You are reliable with consistent attendance.



HOURS:

-- Part time (20 hours) to start, with the option for full time after 30 days.

-- Monday - Friday 10AM-2PM Pacific Standard Time (Tuesday - Saturday 1AM-5AM Philippine Time).



PAYMENT:

-- $4/hour base

-- $20 each qualified appointment that shows up

-- $150 each person who signs up for our services

-- Monthly bonuses



HOW TO APPLY:

-- Do not mention previous jobs that are not specifically related to cold calling B2B.

-- Put "Dial And Smile" as your subject line

-- Copy and paste the following questions and put answers in your application. Please use your own words, and do not use chat GPT or similar:



1) How many months/years of experience do you have specifically cold calling business to business and appointment setting (ONLY put jobs that were entirely cold leads....no warm leads, no inbound calls, etc):



2) What product/service were you offering and to who (please be detailed with this response and clear what you were offering):



3) How many calls per day did you average:



4) How many appointments per week did you average:



5) Did you source your own leads or were you given leads:



6) Do you currently have another job:



7) You call a business and a receptionist answers. They ask, “What is this regarding?”

What do you say to get through to the owner?

(Keep your answer to 1–2 sentences max and write it exactly how you would say it on a real call):



8) What makes you better than other cold callers applying for this role:

title: Billing Coordinator/Case Manager
description:
Billing Coordinator / Case Manager



Desert Bolt Behavioral LLC



Position Summary



Desert Bolt Behavioral LLC is seeking a highly organized, detail-oriented, and compassionate Billing Coordinator / Case Manager to support both administrative revenue-cycle operations and patient care coordination.



This role is responsible for managing insurance verification, billing follow-up, authorizations, claims support, patient communication, scheduling coordination, and case management tasks. The ideal candidate is professional, dependable, comfortable working with patients and families, and able to protect confidential health information at all times.



This position requires strong communication skills, accuracy, discretion, and the ability to work in a fast-paced behavioral health environment.



Key Responsibilities

Billing Coordination



The Billing Coordinator / Case Manager will:



Verify patient insurance benefits before appointments.

Confirm eligibility, copays, deductibles, coinsurance, and prior authorization requirements.

Submit, track, and follow up on claims as needed.

Monitor unpaid claims, denied claims, and rejected claims.

Communicate with insurance companies regarding claim status and payment issues.

Assist with correcting claim errors and resubmissions.

Maintain accurate billing documentation in the EHR system.

Communicate patient financial responsibility clearly and professionally.

Assist with payment collection, payment plans, and patient billing questions.

Coordinate with providers and administrative staff to ensure billing accuracy.

Track authorizations and ensure services are properly approved when required.

Case Management



The employee will also:



Assist patients and families with navigating appointments, referrals, and care coordination.

Help gather required clinical, demographic, and insurance information.

Coordinate communication between patients, providers, pharmacies, schools, outside clinics, and insurance plans when appropriate.

Follow up with patients regarding missed appointments, pending forms, medication-related administrative issues, and required documentation.

Support referral tracking and continuity-of-care needs.

Help ensure patients understand next steps in their care plan.

Identify urgent administrative or clinical concerns and escalate them to the appropriate licensed provider.

Maintain professional, compassionate communication with patients at all times.

Administrative Duties



The Billing Coordinator / Case Manager may also be responsible for:



Answering phone calls and responding to patient messages.

Scheduling and confirming appointments.

Updating patient demographics and insurance information.

Uploading documents into the EHR.

Sending intake forms, consent forms, and follow-up paperwork.

Maintaining organized records and task lists.

Supporting front desk workflows when needed.

Assisting with audits, reports, and internal quality-improvement tasks.

Required Qualifications

High school diploma or equivalent required.

Prior experience in medical billing, behavioral health billing, case management, healthcare administration, or front desk medical office work preferred.

Familiarity with insurance verification, claims, authorizations, copays, deductibles, and denials preferred.

Strong computer and data-entry skills.

Excellent phone etiquette and written communication skills.

Ability to handle confidential information professionally.

Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

Ability to multitask and follow through on pending items.

Professional, calm, and respectful communication style.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in behavioral health, psychiatry, counseling, or outpatient medical settings.

Experience using EHR systems such as Practice Fusion, AdvancedMD, TherapyNotes, SimplePractice, Kareo/Tebra, or similar platforms.

Knowledge of Medicaid, commercial insurance, prior authorizations, and behavioral health billing.

Bilingual skills preferred but not required.

Experience working with children, adolescents, adults, and families in a healthcare setting.

Required Skills and Competencies



The ideal candidate must demonstrate:



Accuracy with billing and insurance information.

Strong follow-up and task-management skills.

Compassionate communication with patients and families.

Ability to remain calm under pressure.

Good judgment when escalating concerns.

Respect for HIPAA and patient privacy.

Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

Professional boundaries and ethical conduct.

Reliability, punctuality, and accountability.

HIPAA and Confidentiality



This position requires strict compliance with HIPAA and all applicable privacy laws. The employee must protect patient information at all times and may not disclose confidential information except as permitted by law and company policy.



Work Environment



This role may involve working in an outpatient behavioral health office, remote administrative setting, or hybrid environment depending on business needs. The employee must be comfortable communicating with patients, families, insurance companies, pharmacies, and clinical staff.



Schedule



Job Type: Full-time or Part-time

Schedule: Monday through Friday, business hours preferred

Work Location: In-person, remote, or hybrid depending on company needs



Compensation



Compensation is based on experience, qualifications, and job responsibilities.

title: Operations & Marketing Associate
description:
We are building a brand house — a company that creates direct-to-consumer (DTC) brands from scratch and grows them into household names across South East Asia and beyond. Think of it as a startup studio for consumer products: we identify category opportunities, develop the products, partner with world-class manufacturers, design the brand and packaging, and own the relationship with the customer from first touch to repeat purchase.



We are hiring 2–3 founding team associates to build it with us. You will have a hand in everything — from sitting across from a factory partner in another country, negotiating an MOQ, to d
 the colour palette for a new brand, to launching it on Shopee and Lazada, to staring at the first week’s sales data and figuring out what to do next. If you are a recent graduate who would rather build something from zero than join a department of fifty, this role is for you.



Our playbook for each brand:

1. Identify product categories where we can build something better, cheaper, or more delightful than what is on the shelf today.

2. Negotiate with manufacturing, packaging, and distribution partners to bring products to life cost-effectively and at quality.

3. Design brands, packaging, and digital experiences that feel premium and considered.

4. Build sales channels that compound — owned e-commerce, regional marketplaces, paid acquisition, organic social, creator partnerships, and eventually retail.

5. Launch first in Malaysia, expand across South East Asia, and take the winners to global markets.



We are deliberately small, deliberately fast-moving, and deliberately broad in what each person owns.



This is one job description for what could be 2 or 3 hires. We have written it as a single role because in a team this small, titles matter less than the appetite to figure things out. In practice, you will lean toward one or two of the following functions based on your strengths and interests, and stretch into the others when the work calls for it.



Operations & Supply Chain: Sourcing products and materials. Vetting and negotiating with suppliers. Managing manufacturing timelines. Coordinating packaging and freight. Setting up warehousing and last-mile distribution in Malaysia and across South East Asia. Building the systems and SOPs that let the company run cleanly as it grows.



Design: Visual identity. Packaging design. Product styling and photography direction. E-commerce storefronts. Marketing assets. Translating each brand’s story into something tangible and beautiful that customers want to put on their counter, share with a friend, or photograph for Instagram.



Brand & Marketing: Brand strategy and positioning. Content and copy. Social media. Influencer and creator partnerships. Performance marketing on Meta, TikTok, and Google. Marketplace optimisation on Shopee and Lazada. Customer research. Post-purchase experience. And the analytics behind all of it.



If you are clearly stronger in one of these areas, you will lead it. If you are equally curious across all three, even better.



What You Will Actually Do in Your First Year

1. Help launch our first 1–3 DTC brands from concept to live storefront.

2. Travel abroad several times a year to visit factories, attend trade fairs, and build long-term relationships with manufacturing partners. Note: For Filipino hires, all your travel costs will be covered, and it is entirely optional to you if you want to gain overseas work exposure.  You can choose to work remotely entirely.

3. Negotiate pricing, MOQs, and payment terms with suppliers — and learn to read between the lines of a quote.

4. Own end-to-end logistics for at least one product line, from PO to customer doorstep.

5. Brief and work alongside designers, photographers, and content creators — and where useful, do the work yourself.

6. Set up and manage our e-commerce stack — Shopify, marketplace listings, payment gateways, fulfilment integrations.

7. Run paid acquisition campaigns and learn what makes a creative work in this region.

8. Talk to customers. Read every review. Run interviews. Turn what you hear into product and brand decisions.

9. Build the dashboards and rituals that let the team see how each brand is really doing.

10. Take on responsibilities we have not even thought of yet, because that is how startups work.



Who We Are Looking For: We are looking for fresh graduates (or near-graduates) with the energy, curiosity, and resilience to build something from nothing. Specific backgrounds matter less than how you think and how you operate. That said, the following will help:

1. A bachelor’s degree in any field — business, design, engineering, communications, the arts, or anything else you have been excited about.

2. Strong written and spoken English. Malay is a plus. Mandarin is a significant plus given how often you will be speaking with manufacturing partners in China.

3. Comfort with numbers — you can build a basic costing model, calculate a margin, and reason about unit economics.

4. Visual literacy — you can tell good design from bad, even if you cannot yet make it yourself.

5. Familiarity with the platforms our customers live on — Instagram, TikTok, Xiaohongshu, Shopee, Lazada — and a real interest in how brands grow on them.

6. A portfolio, side project, internship, club, or anything else that shows you have shipped something to the world before.



If you are missing one or two of the above but have the appetite to learn fast, please apply anyway. We would rather hire someone who is hungry than someone who looks perfect on paper.



Characteristics That Lead to Success Here: We have thought hard about what makes founding-team operators thrive in a business like this. The strongest candidates tend to share these traits:

1. High agency. You do not wait for permission or instructions. When you see a problem, you take a swing at it; if you are stuck, you find someone to ask.

2. Comfort with ambiguity. You are energised, not paralysed, by problems without a clear answer or clean playbook. You are willing to work in pencil, not pen.

3. Bias for shipping. You would rather get version one out and learn from it than spend three more weeks polishing version zero. Good is better than perfect; live is better than good.

4. Owner’s mindset. You think in terms of the whole business, not just your slice. You will pick up trash on the way out of the office, both literally and figuratively.

5. Cross-cultural fluency. You can build a real relationship with a factory owner in Guangzhou and a 22-year-old creator in KL — and you genuinely enjoy both conversations.

6. Taste and judgment. You can tell when a product, an ad, a piece of packaging, or an
 is good — and you can articulate why. Taste, here, is a function of paying attention.

7. Hunger to learn. You read, watch, listen, and talk to people about how this industry actually works. You can quote what you learned from a podcast, a Substack, or a long thread last week.

8. Endurance over sprint pace. Building brands is a multi-year project. You are someone who keeps showing up — through quiet weeks, bad launches, and the inevitable supply-chain headache.

9. Honesty. You tell the truth about the numbers, the timeline, and your own work — to your teammates, to partners, and to yourself.

title: Creative Administrative Assistant
description:
Hi! I’m an artist and author managing multiple creative projects, and I’m looking for a reliable, detail-oriented assistant to help me stay organized and reduce mental load.



This is a part-time role (5–8 hours per week) with potential to grow over time.



Main responsibilities:



* Track my deadlines and organize weekly priorities

* Update my website with new work, articles, and press

* Research opportunities (galleries, speaking, publications)

* Assist with simple outreach and submissions

* Help maintain and update my IMDb profile



Important:

I already create and post my own content, so this role is focused on organization, research, and follow-through—not social media creation.



I’m looking for someone who is:



* Highly organized and detail-oriented

* Reliable and proactive

* Comfortable working independently

* Interested in creative fields (art, writing, film is a plus)



Starting rate is $5/hr with opportunity to increase based on performance.



My goal is simple: to find the most effective way to reduce stress and free up my time so I can focus on creating.



To apply, please:



1. Tell me about your experience with organization or research

title: P/T Bookkeeper
description:
MeetBookkeepers is a New York–based outsourced accounting firm serving beverage alcohol operators, engineering and architecture firms, real estate businesses, and small consulting companies. We function as both embedded accounting departments for our clients and a transactional bookkeeping shop. 



The Role We're hiring a part-time bookkeeper to own day-to-day transaction processing and reconciliation across a small portfolio of client accounts including consulting businesses and a multi-entity beverage alcohol operator. Work is recurring, deadline-driven, and SOP-guided. You do not need to be a CPA. You need to be precise, consistent, and communicative.

This role has room to grow as our client base expands. 



Responsibilities

Transaction coding and categorization across multiple client accounts

Bank and credit card reconciliation on defined cadences

Multi-entity bookkeeping inside QuickBooks Online

Intercompany transaction tagging per client COA structure

Flagging anomalies and unresolved items to team lead before deadlines

Prompt responses to internal team communications; same business-day standard



Required Qualifications

2+ years of bookkeeping experience

QuickBooks Online proficiency; multi-client or multi-entity experience preferred

Experience with inventory-based, distribution, or product businesses

Comfortable managing multiple clients with different chart of accounts structures

Deadline-disciplined and detail-oriented

Reliable internet, quiet working environment



Preferred Qualifications

Experience supporting a US-based outsourced bookkeeping or accounting firm

Accounting degree or active CPA candidate

Familiarity with beverage alcohol, food/beverage, or distribution industry bookkeeping



What We Provide

Full COA documentation for every client before you touch their books

SOPs for all recurring tasks

Direct team lead access for escalations

Structured QA during your ramp period on each new client



What This Is Not

Tax prep, financial analysis, or compliance filing

Autonomous judgment calls on ambiguous coding; escalate, don't guess



Compensation $400–$600/mo USD depending on experience and QBO depth. Hours and scope will grow with firm. Paid monthly via Wise. State your rate expectation in your application.



To Apply Send your CV, a brief description of the types of businesses you've done bookkeeping for, and your monthly rate. Tell us specifically if you have multi-entity QBO experience and whether you've worked with product or distribution businesses.

title: Video Editor (Storytelling / Post-Production)
description:
About BESAS | BE SmartAdmin Services Pty Ltd 



BESAS (BE SmartAdmin Services Pty Ltd) is an Australian-based outsourcing and business support company delivering high-quality remote staffing solutions across creative, finance, and professional services. 



We partner primarily with Australian businesses, helping them scale through highly skilled offshore talent based in the Philippines. Our team operates remotely but works closely with clients to deliver seamless, high-performance support across different industries, including creative production, administration, bookkeeping, and paraplanning services. 



We are a people-first organisation focused on quality, accountability, and long-term client partnerships. Our teams thrive in structured, collaborative environments where performance, creativity, and reliability matter. 



Client Overview 



For this role, the successful candidate will be assigned to a creative Australian visual production company based in Darwin, Northern Territory. The company is a small but highly creative team of under 10 staff, producing impactful visual content across multiple platforms and formats. 



Role Overview  



We are seeking a highly skilled Offline Editor (Video Editor) to join the production team and take ownership of assembling raw footage into compelling, story-driven video content. 



This role focuses on shaping narrative, emotion, and pacing through expert editing—transforming raw footage into structured, engaging visual stories from rough cut through to final locked edit. 



The ideal candidate thrives in storytelling, enjoys working with creative teams, and has a strong eye for timing, rhythm, and narrative clarity. 



Key Responsibilities 



   >  Assemble raw footage into structured story narratives  

   >  Select and refine the best takes to build cohesive sequences  

   >  Develop pacing, rhythm, and emotional flow of video content  

   >  Edit interviews, dialogue-based content, and music-driven visuals  

   >  Progress edits through rough cut, fine cut, and locked cut stages  

   >  Ensure clarity of story, message, and emotional impact  

   >  Collaborate with producers and creatives to refine edits based on feedback  

   >  Interpret creative direction and apply feedback through shared review systems  

   >  Support internal team communication through draft sharing and revisions  



Tools & Software 



   >  Adobe Premiere Pro (primary editing tool)  

   >  Adobe After Effects (supporting motion/graphics integration where needed)  

   >  Other Adobe Creative Cloud tools as required  



Ideal Candidate Profile 



The ideal candidate will bring both technical editing ability and strong storytelling instincts. You will thrive in this role if you: 



>  Have strong experience in video editing and narrative construction  

>  Enjoy working with raw footage and shaping it into meaningful stories  

>  Are confident editing interviews, commercial content, and digital media  

>  Have a strong sense of timing, pacing, and emotional delivery  

>  Are detail-oriented and able to work independently on assigned edits  

>  Communicate well and collaborate effectively with creative teams  

>  Are open to feedback and able to refine edits efficiently  



A portfolio demonstrating editing work is required and will be a key part of the selection process. 



???? Please send your portfolio to: 
 



Working Hours 



   >  Monday to Friday  

   >  7:00 AM – 3:30 PM Philippine Standard Time (PHT)  

   >  Includes a 30-minute unpaid lunch break (preferably around 11:00 AM PHT)  

   >  Aligned with client working hours: 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM ACST (UTC+9:30)  

   >  Lunch alignment: approximately 12:30 PM ACST (UTC+9:30)  



Compensation Structure 



   >  Billable Rate: PhP 350/hour 

        (applies when working directly on client tasks and deliverables)  

   >   Non-Billable Rate: ?200/hour 

         (applies to BESAS internal time such as admin updates, HR catch-ups, company activities, and internal coordination)  



Why Join BESAS 



At BESAS, you will be part of a growing team supporting high-calibre Australian clients in creative and professional industries. You’ll gain exposure to international production workflows while contributing meaningfully to real client projects in a structured, collaborative environment.

title: Outbound Caller
description:
Part time or full time up to you. Seeking one person for now but will need more in future.



I will have a CRM with leads for you to outbound warm calls for people asking for Health Insurance Coverage or a switch in their plan. I will give you a script, and you would call the leads to ask them 5-8 questions to fill out a "Fact Finder" form. The goal would be 25 FFs (Fact Finders) per week or more. This will be during USA business hours of course between 9am and as late as 7pm. I would encourage part time seekers to apply as maybe you have a second job and I will eventually like to be able to get you paid enough that you won't need the second job. 



You would get a small bonus for every FF you submit up to a max of $5 extra per day (if you do more than this we would of course discuss the next bonus for production as you're obviously bringing a higher value to the company, and I will want to pay you for that. Also each confirmed sale will give you a bonus as well which has no limit. Potentially you can make more than double your base salary rate any given day/week/month. I will consider a lower requested salary as priority over higher so that I can build in your bonuses and extra earnings. 



I reward success and when I scale would be adding more VAs and will eventually need a manager role if interested. I also will eventually lease a physical building in the Philippines, which will have a day care center, gym, and cafeteria for employees to use. This project is about 1 year out. 



TO BE CONSIDERED FOR THIS ROLE PLEASE SUBMIT TO ME A VIDEO LINK/CAN BE AUDIO AS WELL BUT EVENTUALLY WE WILL MEET ON ZOOM BRIEFLY OF 60 SECONDS TO HEAR YOUR VOICE AND SEE SOME OF YOUR TONE/SPEAKING/SKILLS. IF YOU DO NOT INCLUDE A VIDEO OR AUDIO RECORDING I WILL NOT RESPOND TO YOUR REQUEST.



NEXT TO SEE IF YOU FOLLOW SIMPLE INSTRUCTIONS SOMEWHERE IN YOUR REPLY INCLUDE YOUR FAVORITE HOBBY OR ACTIVITY OR MAYBE IF YOU'RE STUDYING SOMETHING IN SCHOOL.

title: Wordpress Developer
description:
We’re looking for a seasoned Front End WordPress Developer to join our agency team. This role is a mix of building new sites and improving existing ones, with a strong focus on performance, SEO, and long-term maintainability.



This is not a “click some buttons and ship it” role. We care about clean code, fast load times, and sites that don’t fall apart six months later.



What you’d be working on:



Building new WordPress sites from concept through launch

Improving and maintaining existing WordPress builds

Implementing Advanced Custom Fields (ACF) for flexible, scalable content

Writing custom JavaScript when plugins aren’t the right solution

Working within established themes and frameworks, including Avada and Enfold

Translating designs into responsive, accessible HTML and CSS

Optimizing for Core Web Vitals, PageSpeed, and real-world performance

Reducing bloat from themes, plugins, and unnecessary scripts

Collaborating with SEO and marketing teams to support rankings and conversions



What we’re looking for:



4+ years of professional WordPress development experience

Strong HTML and CSS skills (non-negotiable)

Solid JavaScript experience for front end customization

Deep experience with ACF

Strong understanding of technical and on-page SEO

Proven experience improving WordPress performance

Comfortable working within existing themes and frameworks

Familiarity with Git and collaborative workflows

Ability to work independently and make smart technical decisions



Bonus Points:



Experience with Avada, Enfold, or similar theme frameworks

Experience integrating APIs or third-party services

Gutenberg block experience

Working knowledge of PHP

Experience and comfort juggling multiple projects





If this sounds like your kind of work, we’d love to talk. Please reply with a few examples of WordPress sites you’ve worked on and a brief note on your role in each.

description:

We are seeking a **highly experienced and detail-oriented CPA** based in the Philippines to manage accounting and financial reporting across multiple business entities and personal finances. This is a **long-term, part-time role** with consistent work and opportunities to grow.



---



What You’ll Handle:



* Bookkeeping and reconciliations for an **entertainment business (Tiki Bar with event-based and daily sales activity)**

* Full accounting for a **real estate business** (rentals, flips, leasing, and sales)

* Weekly, monthly, and year-to-date **budget vs. actual reporting** for both businesses

* **Monthly sales tax preparation and filing** for the Tiki Bar

* Financial tracking and setup for **new startup ventures**

* Owner’s **personal accounting**, including expense categorization, reimbursements, and budgeting

* **Ad hoc projects** such as audit prep, system transitions, year-end reporting, etc.

* Payroll coordination and oversight (via Gusto or similar)

* Communication with U.S.-based ownership via
 and occasional Zoom



---



Requirements:

* **CPA license (Philippines)**

* Minimum **5 years of full-time accounting experience**

* Experience in **public accounting** or as a supervisor/manager preferred

* Strong understanding of **accrual and cash accounting**

* Proficiency with **QuickBooks Online**

* Strong analytical and reporting skills

* Comfortable working **partially during U.S. business hours**

* Organized, detail-oriented, proactive, and trustworthy



---



Hours & Pay:

* **Part-time**: \~15–20 hours/week

* **Pay:** 15,000–20,000/month

  Starting rate based on experience, with raises based on performance and reliability

* Paid twice per month via Wise

* Flexible working hours as long as deadlines are consistently met

* **Note**: Weekly hours typically range from **12–18 hours**, but may be higher during onboarding or cleanup work



---



Bonus Points If You Have:

* Experience with **U.S.-based hospitality, entertainment, or real estate clients**

* Familiarity with POS systems, property management software, or U.S. tax workflows

* Background in supporting **event-based businesses or multi-entity structures**



---



**To Apply:**

Send an
/message with the subject line: **“CPA Application – \[Your Name]”** and include:



* Your resume

* Summary of relevant accounting experience

* Your available working hours

* A short explanation of why you’re a strong fit for this role

* A link or a attachment to a short video introduction relating your experience to the role is required

title: Meta Ads Specialist for Texas Real Estate Buyer Leads
description:
Meta Ads Specialist for Texas Real Estate Buyer Leads



We are hiring a Meta Ads Specialist to help generate qualified buyer interest for a Texas single-family rental portfolio sale.



This is not a general social media role.

This is not a content posting role.

This is not a branding-only role.

This is not a role for someone who only knows how to boost posts.



We need someone who understands how to run
 and Instagram ads for real estate, work inside Meta’s Housing Special Ad Category, and generate serious buyer conversations.



Housing-related ads on Meta have audience and targeting restrictions, so this role requires someone who already understands how compliant real estate advertising works.



About the Portfolio



We are selling a portfolio of 19 single-family homes in the Texarkana, Wake Village, and Nash, Texas area, excluding one higher-priced outlier.



The portfolio includes:



8 leased, cash-flowing properties

5 vacant, move-in-ready properties

6 vacant properties needing repairs or final work before move-in

Some vacant properties are also being leased up



Most of the portfolio is in the $89,000 to $249,000 price range.



We are open to selling:



Individual homes

Small groups

Bundles

The full portfolio



Your role is to help us use Meta ads to generate qualified buyer conversations, not just cheap leads.



Responsibilities



You may be responsible for:



Reviewing the portfolio and recommending the best Meta ad angles

Building compliant campaigns under the Housing category

Writing strong ad copy for real estate buyers and investors

Recommending creatives, hooks, and offers

Launching and managing campaigns

Testing lead forms, landing pages, retargeting, or a mixed funnel

Helping improve lead quality

Tracking campaign performance

Reporting clearly on results

Helping connect paid traffic with follow-up so leads are not wasted

Making recommendations based on data and lead quality

What We Want



You should already have experience with:



 and Instagram ads

Real estate paid ads

Housing Special Ad Category campaigns

Lead generation

Conversion-focused ad copy

Retargeting

Performance reporting

Conversion tracking

Lead quality improvement

Strong Plus If You

Have worked on real estate investor or buyer campaigns

Understand how to get results even with housing targeting restrictions

Know how to attract serious buyers, landlords, or investors

Can write copy that creates qualified conversations

Can show campaign proof or examples

Can start quickly

Can work independently

Can communicate clearly

Not a Fit If



Please do not apply if:



You are mainly a general social media manager

You only create content but do not run paid campaigns

You have no real estate Meta ads experience

You do not understand Housing-category rules

You focus only on impressions, clicks, or cheap leads

You cannot explain how you would improve lead quality

You need heavy hand-holding

You send generic applications without reading the post

To Apply



Please send:



A short intro

Your direct experience with Meta ads for real estate

Your experience with Housing Special Ad Category campaigns

2 to 3 relevant campaign examples

What you would test first for this portfolio

Sample ad angles or copy ideas

Whether you would use instant forms, landing pages, retargeting, or a mixed approach

What your first 7 days would look like

Your availability and time zone

Required Screening Exercise



In your application, include one example of how you would position this portfolio on Meta while staying compliant with Housing Special Ad Category rules.



Please answer:



What buyer angle would you test first and why?

Would you start with instant forms, a landing page, retargeting, or a mixed funnel?

What would your first ad hook say?

How would you qualify leads so we are not wasting time with low-intent inquiries?

What would you avoid doing because of Housing-category restrictions?



Applications that do not answer these questions will not be considered.



Compensation



Open to base pay plus performance bonus based on fit and results.



We are also open to a 7-day paid trial for the right candidate.



We are looking for someone who can use Meta ads seriously and intelligently to help generate real buyer interest for a Texas real estate portfolio.

title: AI Product Lister & Image Specialist (4K Visuals) — Curved Spirit
description:
At Curved Spirit, we are building more than a store — we are building a premium visual brand experience.



We are looking for a top-tier AI Product Lister & Image Specialist who can transform our current product images into high-end, 4K, conversion-focused visuals using advanced AI tools like Nano Banana Pro or similar.



This role is for someone who understands that images sell the product — and knows how to make them look premium, realistic, and irresistible.



Your Role



You will take our existing product images and elevate them into next-level visuals that feel like luxury brand campaigns.



You don’t just edit — you recreate, enhance, and upgrade.



What You’ll Do



Upgrade existing product images into 4K, high-end visuals

Use tools like Nano Banana Pro (or similar AI models) to generate premium imagery

Create studio-quality product shots (clean, minimal, luxury style)

Develop lifestyle images that match our brand identity

Ensure images look ultra-realistic and conversion-focused

Apply correct product details (fit, material, compression, shape) into visuals

Maintain consistency across all product listings

Optimize images for Shopify, Meta Ads, and product pages

Continuously improve visual quality and test new creative approaches



What We’re Looking For



Strong experience with AI image generation (REQUIRED)

Experience with tools like Nano Banana Pro, Midjourney, DALL·E, or similar

Proven ability to create high-quality, realistic product images

Experience in fashion or shapewear (STRONG PLUS)

Understanding of how images impact conversions

Ability to recreate products accurately and realistically

Strong eye for detail, lighting, textures, and fit

Creative mindset — you bring new ideas and concepts

Ability to work fast and deliver consistently



What Makes You a Perfect Fit



You know the difference between basic AI images and premium visuals

You can make products look like they belong in a luxury campaign

You understand how to show fit, shaping, and body confidence

You think beyond instructions and bring creative direction

You are obsessed with quality and realism



Bonus Points



Experience with shapewear brands

Experience creating before/after visuals

Experience with UGC-style lifestyle images

Understanding of e-commerce product listing optimization

Experience working with Shopify stores



Why Work With Us



Fast-growing premium shapewear brand

Focus on quality, branding, and performance

Opportunity to shape the visual identity of the brand

Long-term collaboration potential

How to Apply



Start your application with: VELVET



Include:



Portfolio of AI-generated images (REQUIRED)

Before/after examples (if available)

Tools you use

Your experience in fashion/shapewear

Your availability

title: Wedding Video Editor, Luxury Weddings
description:
Position Title: Wedding Video Editor (Part Time, 6 Hours/Day)

Company: Brette Morgan Films

Location: Remote

Position Type: Part Time (Approximately 30 hours per week)



Overview

Brette Morgan Films (
) is seeking a dedicated and detail oriented video editor to join our team. This role is focused entirely on post production, crafting wedding films that are both emotionally resonant and technically refined.



You will be responsible for editing wedding highlight films, full documentary edits, and short form social media reels. This position requires a strong sense of storytelling, pacing, and an understanding of how to preserve authentic 
nts while delivering a polished final product.



Key Responsibilities



* Edit 1-2 minute trailers 

* Edit 5 to 8 minute cinematic wedding highlight films

* Edit full length documentary wedding films (2 to 3 hours, lightly edited for continuity and audio clarity)

* Create short form vertical reels for social media (30 to 90 seconds)

* Sync multi camera footage and external audio using timecode and waveform 

* Organize and manage large volumes of footage in an efficient workflow

* Color correct and apply color grading

* Mix and clean audio for speeches, vows, and ceremony

* Incorporate music, dialogue, and natural sound in a cohesive way

* Export and deliver files to Dropbox 

* Follow established brand style, pacing, and storytelling guidelines



?



Qualifications



* Willing to collaborate and work as a team

* Strong proficiency in DaVinci Resolve or Adobe Premiere Pro

* Experience editing wedding films or similar long form event content

* Strong understanding of storytelling, pacing, and emotional timing and non-chronological edits. 

* Experience working with multi-cam timelines and syncing audio

* Basic color correction and audio enchantment skills

* Ability to meet deadlines and manage multiple projects simultaneously

* Strong attention to detail and organization

* Strong proficiency in English 



?



Preferred (Not Required)



* Experience with 
 or similar review platforms

* Familiarity with luxury wedding market aesthetics

* Experience editing for social media platforms like Instagram

?

Work Schedule

* Approximately 6 hours per day

* Flexible schedule with required turnaround deadlines

* Occasional priority edits during peak wedding season for trailer edits 

?

Compensation

* Based on experience

* Paid hourly

?

What We Value

Our films are designed to feel timeless, grounded, and centered on family and connection. We are looking for the right editor who understands restraint, allowing 
nts to breathe while still shaping a compelling narrative. Our films have a strong emphasis on family and including family members in the films. Our films also have a huge emphasis on audio. We use natural audio, speeches and timecode lav mics to make sure most clips have natural audio from the day. 

Our films are all non-chronological and I you will ample time to work on each project. We shoot less than 15 weddings per year therefore you will have weeks to carefully craft each film. 

?



How to Apply

Please submit:

* Portfolio of wedding or event films (highlight and full length if available)

* A short introduction about your editing style and experience and why you would be a good fit for our small US based brand. 

* I will send a test project for a 1-2 minute teaser film and then a formal interview following.

title: Email Operations Manager — DTC E-Commerce Agency (Remote, Full-Time or Part-Time)
description:
Email Marketing Operations Manager — Klaviyo / E-Commerce



We are an
 marketing agency that manages campaigns and automated flows for fast-growing e-commerce brands across health, supplements, dental, beauty, and shapewear.



From the start of June, you will be working across four active e-commerce brand accounts, helping to manage the full
 marketing operations for each brand.



We are looking for someone who can take ownership of the operational side of our
 accounts, so the founder can focus on growth, sales and high-level strategy.



This is not a basic VA role.



We need someone who genuinely understands e-commerce
 marketing, Klaviyo, customer journeys, campaign planning, performance data, design direction and the full process of getting high-converting
s from idea to launch.



It is good to make a nice-looking
, but that is not enough.



The goal is to create
s that get opened, clicked and purchased from.



You will be responsible for:



Managing campaign and flow operations across four e-commerce brand accounts

Monitoring campaign and flow performance inside Klaviyo

Reviewing open rates, click rates, placed order rates, purchase rates and overall revenue performance

Identifying what is working, what is underperforming and what needs improving

Planning monthly campaign calendars for each brand

Knowing what
s need to go out, when they need to go out and why

Creating clear, detailed briefs for the graphic designer

Making sure the designer understands the copy, customer journey, offer, layout, CTA placement and overall
 objective

Reviewing completed designs and checking whether they are built to convert, not just look nice

Slicing approved Figma designs and building them cleanly inside Klaviyo

Setting up campaigns, segments, links, discounts and scheduling

Checking
s properly before launch, including mobile view, links, layout, CTAs and copy

Keeping deadlines, flow updates and account tasks organised

Spotting problems before they become problems

The Briefing Side Is Important



A big part of this role is knowing how to brief the graphic designer properly.



You need to understand how to explain:



What the
 is about

What the customer needs to feel or understand

Where the customer is in their journey

What pain point, offer or angle the
 is focused on

Where the CTA should be placed

What the main click action should be

How the design should guide the reader towards the purchase

What sections are needed and why

How the
 should be structured for conversion



We do not want random pretty
s.



We want
s that have a reason behind every section.



Every headline, image, CTA, product block, review, offer and benefit should have a purpose.



What We Need From You



You must have:



Strong Klaviyo experience across campaigns, flows, segments and analytics

Experience working with e-commerce/DTC brands

The ability to read
 performance data and understand what action needs to be taken

A good understanding of open rate, click rate, placed order rate, purchase rate and revenue per recipient

Experience briefing or managing designers/creatives

Confidence working with Figma designs and building
s in Klaviyo

Good attention to detail when checking links, layout, mobile view, copy and scheduling

The ability to work independently without needing constant direction

A strong understanding of what makes an
 convert, not just look good

Awareness of trending topics, design trends, seasonal campaigns and what is currently working in e-commerce



You should be able to look at what is happening in the world, what is trending online, what customers are reacting to, and help turn that into relevant campaign ideas for our brands.



Growth Opportunity



This is a strong opportunity for someone who wants to grow into a serious e-commerce
 marketing expert.



You will be working directly inside real brand accounts, learning how campaigns, flows, offers, customer journeys and creative decisions affect actual revenue.



You will also be taught and guided along the way, especially around strategy, creative direction, campaign structure, customer journey thinking and how to improve
 performance.



The more you improve, the more responsibility you will be given.



The goal is for you to grow with the agency long-term.



This Role Is Not For You If



Please do not apply if:



You have never worked in e-commerce
 marketing

You only have basic admin or VA experience

You need constant instructions for every task

You cannot look at Klaviyo data and explain what it means

You cannot build
s from Figma into Klaviyo

You do not understand customer journeys

You only care about making
s look nice, without thinking about clicks and purchases

You are not comfortable taking responsibility for deadlines and account operations

Pay & Growth



Starting pay is $500 per month, depending on experience and ability.



This will be reviewed based on performance. If you prove you can take ownership, manage accounts properly and help the agency run smoother, the role can grow up to $1,500 per month as responsibilities increase.



This can be full-time or part-time depending on the right fit.



We are looking for someone long-term who can grow with the agency as we scale.



How To Apply



Application subject line: The Flash



No exceptions. Applications without this subject line will not be reviewed.



When applying, please include examples of previous work, results, Klaviyo screenshots, campaign examples, flow examples or anything that shows your experience.



For a quicker response, message on WhatsApp:



plus four four, seven eight eight eight, zero seven three, eight eight eight

title: Virtual Assistant to the Founders
description:
We are a fast-growing CRM and automation agency specializing in Salesforce and HubSpot implementations. We’re hiring a Virtual Assistant to work directly with our two co-founders. The mission of this role is simple: give the founders back their minutes. Every task you take off their plate is time they can spend building the business, serving clients, and closing deals.

This is not a passive support role. You’ll be a force multiplier — someone who anticipates needs, takes initiative, and operates with the judgment of an extension of the founders themselves.



What You’ll Be Doing

You’ll own a wide range of administrative and operational responsibilities, including:





-Task management and execution across multiple areas of the business, from internal operations to client-facing follow-ups

-Calendar management for both founders, including scheduling, rescheduling, prioritizing meetings, and protecting focus time

-Inbox and message triage, including flagging urgent
s, drafting responses, and ensuring nothing falls through the cracks

-Client communication support, including responding to messages, following up on outstanding items, and keeping conversations moving

-Sales lead management, including monitoring inbound leads, flagging high-priority opportunities, and ensuring timely responses

-Ad hoc projects that come up in a fast-moving startup environment, ranging from research to documentation to vendor coordination





What We’re Looking For



-Excellent written and verbal English. You’ll be communicating with clients, prospects, and partners directly, often on behalf of the founders.

-Strong attention to detail. Small mistakes in calendars,
s, and client communications create big problems. You catch things before they become issues.

-Comfort with a fast-paced startup environment. Priorities shift, new tasks come in throughout the day, and you’ll need to stay organized when things move quickly.

-Strong sense of ownership. You don’t wait to be told what to do twice. You follow up, follow through, and close loops.

-Good judgment. You’ll be making small decisions on behalf of the founders constantly. We need someone who knows when to act and when to ask.





Nice to Have



-Previous experience as an executive assistant or virtual assistant.

-Familiarity with Google Workspace, Slack, and CRM tools (HubSpot or Salesforce experience is a plus but not required)

-Experience supporting founders or executives in a service or agency business





Why This Role Matters

The two founders of AutomationFix are deeply involved in client delivery, sales, and building the agency. Every minute they spend on calendar logistics or inbox triage is a minute they’re not spending on the work that grows the business. Your success in this role is measured by one thing: how much of their time you give back to them.

title: Senior Procurement Specialist | Hiring ASAP
description:
Full-Time | Remote 



Reports to: Chief Operating Officer



About the role

We are a fast-growing pet products company selling direct-to-consumer and through retail channels. Our products include manufactured and finished goods sourced primarily from China, with additional vendors in the US.



This is a newly created role — the first dedicated procurement hire in our company. The successful candidate will consolidate purchasing currently spread across multiple people, establish supplier relationships, create spend visibility, and build the systems and discipline that will support our growth.



Key responsibilities

• Consolidate all purchasing activity into a single, managed process covering raw materials, components, and finished goods

• Own the full supplier lifecycle: identification, vetting, onboarding, negotiation, and ongoing relationship management

• Manage USD 10–14M in annual spend across the supplier base, primarily in China

• Negotiate pricing, lead times, payment terms, and volume contracts with existing and new suppliers

• Issue and track purchase orders end-to-end, from requisition to receipt

• Align purchasing with inventory targets using our WMS (Finale Inventory) and demand planning inputs

• Build and maintain an Approved Vendor List (AVL) and supplier scorecard

• Identify cost savings opportunities and report on spend vs. budget to leadership

• Coordinate with freight forwarders and manage import logistics for inbound shipments from Asia

• Proactively flag supply chain risks and develop contingency plans



Required qualifications:

• Minimum 5 years of experience in procurement, sourcing, or supply chain management

• Proven experience sourcing from Chinese factories or trading companies

• Strong written and spoken English communication skills

• Demonstrated experience building or significantly improving a purchasing process

• Ability to work independently in a fast-paced, low-structure environment

• Solid understanding of import logistics, Incoterms, and freight coordination

• Prior experience working for a US-based or global company with remote/async work expectations



Preferred qualifications:

• Experience with WMS or inventory management tools (Finale Inventory training provided)

• Background in consumer products, pet products, or e-commerce supply chains

• Familiarity with quality control processes for manufactured goods

• CPSM or CSCP certification

• Experience configuring an ERP or procurement module

• Mandarin language skills (written or spoken)



Work setup:

This is a fully remote, asynchronous-first position. Written communication is the default. You will document decisions, maintain purchasing status in shared trackers, and communicate proactively without relying on calls.



The Philippines time zone (UTC+8) aligns with Chinese business hours, enabling real-time supplier communication during your regular workday. You will have a defined spend authority threshold for routine purchases and will report directly to the COO with a weekly async check-in.



90-day success milestones:

• All active suppliers identified and documented in a centralized supplier register

• All purchasing activity consolidated under this role

• A standardized purchase order process established and consistently followed

• Full spend visibility by category delivered to leadership

• Top supplier contracts reviewed with renegotiation initiated on a minimum of three



HOW TO APPLY:

Please submit your resume via Google Drive link to be CONSIDERED for this position.
