title: Marketing Assistant
description:
Title: Marketing Assistant — Video Editing (Virtual)

Reports To: Karly, Digital Marketing Specialist

Location: Remote, Philippines (Austin, TX business hours overlap required)

Type: Full-time contractor

Compensation: $1,300 – $1,500/month USD, plus 13th-month pay paid in December, prorated by tenure.

Start Date: ~30 days

 

 

============================================================

ABOUT BRAMLETT PARTNERS

============================================================

 

Bramlett Partners is the fastest-growing independent residential real estate brokerage in Austin, TX. We support approximately 148 agents who close 1,300+ transactions and over $775M in volume annually. Our operating philosophy: amazing client service drives 5-star reviews and referrals. Technology, content, and systems are how we scale that standard without adding complexity.

 

We are a technology-forward brokerage. Every tea
mber is expected to use AI daily to work faster, smarter, and more independently.

 

 

============================================================

ROLE SUMMARY

============================================================

 

You will be the video editing engine behind Bramlett Partners' content marketing. You report directly to Karly and collaborate in real time with her on every project. Your job is execution: take raw interview footage, B-roll, and photos and turn them into clean, story-driven videos that get our agents and the Austin neighborhoods we serve in front of the right audiences.

 

Beyond video, you will also produce supporting graphic design assets in Canva — video cover images, award and publication-feature posts, and brand-aligned carousels — using Karly's brand guide as your reference. Video editing is the primary role; design is a meaningful secondary responsibility, not an afterthought.

 

This is a hands-on creative role. Karly is currently doing all of this editing and design herself, which means she is in the weeds instead of focused on grander marketing campaigns. Your success is measured by how much creative work you take off her plate without dropping quality. By day 90, the goal is that Karly is reviewing your cuts and designs, not making them.

 

This role requires good judgment and taste. Transcripts, AI captions, and editing tools are starting points — they are not decision-makers. You will be expected to feel pacing, hear when a cut lands, and recognize when an interview 
nt needs to breathe versus when it needs to be tightened. If you have edited a lot of video and have strong instincts about story, this role is for you. If you are looking for a checklist-execution job, this is not the right fit.

 

 

SEE OUR WORK

------------

Before applying, study our existing video and design work:

 

- Instagram: 

- YouTube: 

 

This is the visual standard, tone, and pacing you will be matching and elevating. If you cannot deliver work at or above this level, this is not the right role for you.

 

 

============================================================

SCOPE AND PHASING

============================================================

 

PHASE 1: DAYS 1-30 (ORIGIN STORIES)

- Learn Karly's editing style, prebuilt templates, and brand standards inside and out

- Take ownership of agent origin story edits — interview cuts, B-roll layering, music, captions

- Deliver one published-quality origin story per week by day 30

- Begin producing video cover images and basic graphic assets in Canva using the brand guide

- Daily video check-ins with Karly to review cuts, get feedback, and learn the judgment model

- Get fluent with CapCut, Epidemic Sound, Canva, and Karly's existing template library

 

PHASE 2: DAYS 31-60 (ADD BUSINESS PROFILES)

- Take over business profile editing (interview + B-roll). Target 3 published profiles per month

- Begin owning the full creative pipeline for origin stories — Karly reviews final, not first cuts

- Take ownership of recurring graphic design work: video covers, award/feature posts, "about us" carousels

- Identify recurring B-roll needs and flag gaps for future filming

- Daily check-ins continue but shorten as you demonstrate independence

 

PHASE 3: DAYS 61-90 (ADD NEIGHBORHOOD SPOTLIGHTS)

- Take over neighborhood spotlight edits (combining multiple business profiles + neighborhood B-roll)

- Deliver 1 neighborhood spotlight per month

- Earned autonomy on origin stories — Karly reviews only when you flag for review

- Own the design pipeline end-to-end for recurring formats; Karly reviews one-offs

- Daily check-ins transition to weekly 1:1s

- (Stretch) Begin shadowing podcast editing in Riverside if Karly d
 to shift ownership of that workstream

 

 

============================================================

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

============================================================

 

ORIGIN STORIES

- What you own: Edit agent origin story videos from interview footage + B-roll + photos. Cut to a clean narrative arc, layer B-roll, score with music, add captions and on-screen elements.

- Success metric: One origin story published per week. Day-30 target: own this end-to-end.

 

BUSINESS PROFILES

- What you own: Edit local business profile videos (interview + B-roll). Match tone of the business, build a story, deliver social-ready cuts.

- Success metric: 3 business profiles published per month. Day-60 target: own this end-to-end.

 

NEIGHBORHOOD SPOTLIGHTS

- What you own: Combine multiple business profiles + neighborhood B-roll into a longer-form spotlight piece. Strong pacing, clear story, music that elevates.

- Success metric: 1 neighborhood spotlight per month. Day-90 target: own this end-to-end.

 

GRAPHIC DESIGN (CANVA)

- What you own: Produce supporting design assets — video cover images, award and publication-feature posts, "about us" carousels, and one-off brand-aligned graphics. Recurring formats use templates; one-offs are built from scratch using the brand guide.

- Success metric: Designs match brand standards on first pass. Karly approves rather than rebuilds.

 

B-ROLL + ASSET MANAGEMENT

- What you own: Review raw footage, log usable B-roll, organize project files in Drive so the team can find anything fast.

- Success metric: Zero lost or unfindable assets. Project files organized to a documented standard.

 

CAPTIONS + ON-SCREEN ELEMENTS

- What you own: Generate AI captions in CapCut, match Karly's prebuilt template, add lower-thirds and on-screen graphics consistent with brand.

- Success metric: Captions are accurate, on-brand, and require minimal Karly edits.

 

MUSIC + SOUND DESIGN

- What you own: Select and customize tracks (Epidemic Sound) that match tone and elevate the edit. Cut/loop tracks cleanly.

- Success metric: Music choices land without rework. Audio levels mixed cleanly across dialogue, B-roll, and music.

 

 

============================================================

AI PROFICIENCY REQUIREMENTS

============================================================

 

This is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have. Every member of the Bramlett Partners team uses AI daily. You will be evaluated on your ability to use AI to work faster, generate better first drafts, and elevate your creative output.

 

MINIMUM EXPECTATIONS:

- Proficient with Claude or ChatGPT for transcript review, story structuring, copy drafting, and creative ideation

- Able to write effective prompts that produce usable first drafts (not just "write me a caption")

- Comfortable using AI to summarize long interview footage and identify the strongest soundbites

- Willing to experiment with new AI tools and creative workflows without hand-holding

 

WITHIN 90 DAYS, YOU SHOULD BE ABLE TO:

- Use Claude to scan interview transcripts and propose narrative outlines for Karly's review

- Build simple Claude Projects with reference material for recurring video formats

- Suggest AI-powered creative or workflow improvements to Karly

 

 

============================================================

REQUIREMENTS

============================================================

 

MUST HAVE

- Strong demonstrated video editing portfolio. We will watch your work.

- Strong storytelling instincts and a clear sense of pacing — particularly for interview-based content where the subject may be awkward on camera. You can find the story and cut to it.

- Proficiency in CapCut, Adobe Premiere Pro, or similar professional editing software

- Comfort with B-roll layering, slow-mo treatments, and frame-rate work (CapCut: slow B-roll to .70 with optimal smooth — or equivalent technique in your editor of choice)

- Music selection and sound design competence — familiarity with Epidemic Sound or similar a plus

- Proficiency in Canva. You will build video cover images, social posts, award and feature graphics, and brand-aligned carousels weekly. Brand guide will be provided after screening.

- Excellent written and spoken English. You will collaborate with Karly in real time via phone and video calls.

- Demonstrated AI proficiency (Claude, ChatGPT, or equivalent). You will be tested in the interview.

- Reliable internet, professional home office setup, and a workstation capable of handling video editing workloads

- Ability to overlap with Austin, TX business hours (CT/UTC-6) for a minimum of 6 hours daily

- Strong attention to detail. Captions, lower-thirds, and on-screen elements need to be accurate and on-brand.

 

NICE TO HAVE

- Experience editing real estate, lifestyle, or brand documentary content

- Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, or other professional design software experience

- Familiarity with Riverside (we currently use it for podcast editing only — open to expanding ownership of that workstream)

- Experience with motion graphics, lower-thirds templates, or basic After Effects work

- Color correction and grading experience

- Experience with social-first vertical formats (Reels, TikTok, Shorts) in addition to long-form

 

DISQUALIFIERS

We will not move forward with candidates who:

- Do not have a video editing portfolio we can review

- Cannot present professionally on camera with clear, understandable English (Karly collaborates with you in real time — voice and presence matter)

- Have no demonstrated experience using AI tools in a work context

- Require constant direction. This role demands creative judgment within Karly's brand guardrails.

- Cannot commit to consistent Austin business hours overlap

 

 

============================================================

AUTHORITY AND DECISION RIGHTS

============================================================

 

- First-pass cuts (origin stories, business profiles): Full authority. Deliver complete first cut for Karly review.

- Final cut sign-off: Karly approves all final cuts in first 60 days. Earned autonomy on origin stories after day 60.

- Music selection: Full authority on track selection. Karly can veto.

- B-roll selection: Full authority. Karly reviews on first cut.

- Graphic design (recurring templated formats): Full authority on first draft. Karly reviews before publish.

- Graphic design (one-off pieces, awards, features): Karly approves before publish in first 90 days.

- Anything published to brokerage social channels: Karly approves before publish. Always.

- Schedule changes, scope changes, new series ideas: Propose to Karly. Do not implement unilaterally.

 

 

============================================================

TOOLS YOU WILL USE

============================================================

 

- CapCut — Primary editor. Karly's preferred tool. Used for all short-form and most long-form work. (Learn by Week 1)

- Epidemic Sound — Music library. Custom track length and flow. (Learn by Week 1)

- Canva — Primary design tool. All graphic design work — covers, posts, carousels, one-off graphics — is built here against the Bramlett brand guide. (Learn by Week 1)

- Riverside — Podcast editing platform. Familiarity is a plus — podcast ownership may shift to this role over time. (Learn by Week 4, familiarity)

- Claude / Claude Projects — AI-assisted prompts for transcript review, copy drafting, and creative direction. Karly will share her Strategy, Design Inspo, and Copy projects. (Learn by Week 1)

- Google Workspace — Drive (footage + project files), Docs, Sheets. (Learn by Week 1)

 

 

============================================================

REPORTING AND COMMUNICATION

============================================================

 

DAYS 1-90

- Daily video check-in with Karly (15-30 minutes) to review cuts in progress, get feedback, and align on priorities

- Daily async written summary at end of your workday: what you completed, what is in progress, what is blocked

- Real-time availability during overlap hours for quick feedback loops on cuts in progress

- Escalation protocol: if unsure, ask. Default to over-communicating in the first 90 days.

 

AFTER 90 DAYS (EARNED AUTONOMY)

- Weekly 1:1 video call with Karly (30 minutes)

- Daily async written check-in continues

 

 

============================================================

WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

============================================================

 

AT 30 DAYS

- You are publishing one origin story per week without quality issues

- You are fluent in CapCut, Karly's templates, Canva, and Epidemic Sound

- Karly is spending less time editing origin stories and producing video covers herself

- You have shown clear creative judgment on at least 4 published edits

 

AT 90 DAYS

- You own origin stories, business profiles, and neighborhood spotlights end-to-end

- You own recurring graphic design formats end-to-end; Karly reviews one-offs only

- Karly is reviewing your final cuts and designs, not making them

- You have proposed at least 2 creative or workflow improvements (using AI or otherwise)

- Karly considers you a creative collaborator she does not need to micromanage

- Daily check-ins have transitioned to weekly 1:1s

 

AT 6 MONTHS

- You are a trusted creative on the Bramlett Partners marketing team

- Karly is fully focused on grander campaigns because the recurring video and design pipeline runs without her in the weeds

- You are experimenting with new formats, AI-augmented workflows, and creative ideas without being asked

- (Possible) You have taken over podcast editing in Riverside if scope expanded in that direction

 

 

============================================================

HOW TO APPLY

============================================================

 

To apply, please complete the TestGorilla assessments here:



 

YOUR APPLICATION SHOULD INCLUDE:

- Your resume

- A portfolio link with at least 3 examples of interview-based video editing work (origin stories, brand documentary, profile pieces preferred) and 2-3 examples of graphic design work (Canva or any tool — social posts, covers, carousels, marketing graphics). Tell us in 1-2 sentences what you did on each piece.

 

Applications without a portfolio (video + design) will not be reviewed.

 

PROCESS:

- Step 1: Complete the assessments and submit your application materials.

- Step 2: Shortlisted candidates are invited to a ~15-minute screener call with our hiring team.

- Step 3: After the screener, finalists complete a trial project (real editing brief and a Canva design brief, source materials provided) so we can see how you actually work.

- Step 4: Final interview with leadership team and offer.

title: Technical Writer
description:
A US-based consulting company is looking to hire a hard-working and self-motivated Filipino Technical Writer to create and document processes and procedures for our clients. Impeccable mastery of the English language (both written and verbal) is a must. 



This role is 100% work from home.



Your responsibilities will include but not be limited to:



• Map process flows based on a recording of the consultant interviewing the client. This includes creating flow charts and diagrams. 

• Write high-quality, clear, easy-to-understand step-by-step procedures based on a recording of an interview of a client or videos submitted directly by clients on how they perform certain tasks.

• Organize the material in a cloud-based process documentation software or Learning Management System software. 

• Use videos, screenshots, links, etc to provide a balance between written material and demonstration of the material and to ensure full understanding. 

• Identify points of improvement and communicate it clearly to the consultant you are working with 

• Use our internal systems to communicate about their work and report daily on progress. 





About You:



• 3+ years of experience in technical writing, creating instructions, creating flow charts, process mapping, and process improvement background. 

• Ability to translate highly technical information into easily understandable information

• Very good understanding of the business world and high familiarity with common terms 

• Experience in process documentation and process improvement 

• Ability to duplicate what is said and translate it into writing

• Excellent mastery of the English language including grammar, punctuation and spelling. 

• Good mastery of MS Products (Word, Excel and PowerPoint) and Google suite 

• Detail-oriented, efficient and concise

• Ability to critical thinking and problem-solving





Compensation: 



• Competitive salary

• Training and mentorship

• Awesome culture and work-from-home set-up

• SSS, Philhealth, and Pag-ibig

• Healthcare (HMO)

• 13th Month Pay

• Paid incentive leave 

• Yearly performance review





How to apply:



Please fill out this application form: 
. We will only respond to candidates that meet the above qualifications.

title: Creative Video Editor for Cinematic Visualization Project (Emotional / Realistic) – NEEDED ASAP
description:
ETA - THIS POSITION WAS POSTED BEFORE, BUT THE APPLICANTS WERE NOT ABLE TO COMPLETE THE TASK PROPERLY. SO I AM HIRING AGAIN FROM SCRATCH.



I WILL EXPECT SAMPLE WORK TO BE DONE BEFOREHAND, WHICH WILL BE PAID ACCORDING TO THE RULES OF OnlineJobs.ph.



PLEASE DO NOT CONTACT IF YOU DO NOT AGREE WITH THE PRICING OR IF YOU DO NOT UNDERSTAND WHAT I AM LOOKING FOR.



I am looking for a creative and skilled person to make a video for a personal visualization project.



The goal of this video is to show a story as if it is happening in real life, so it feels emotional, immersive, and believable.



This is very important because I am using this video for manifestation purposes. The stronger the visualization and the more real it feels, the more powerful it is. The video should help me feel like the situation is actually happening in real life, not just something imagined.



This is a one-time project, but it requires someone who is very creative and professional. You may need to create or find images, design backgrounds, or set up scenes yourself.



The video should feel real and polished, like the story is actually happening.



I am looking for a video that helps me imagine a scenario clearly and emotionally. It should show a sequence of events with music, images, or clips that tell a story.



The focus is on how it feels, not just visuals.



You need to use your creativity to bring everything together, including:



Backgrounds

Scenes

Flow of the story

What You Will Do

Make a video or montage that tells a story visually

Use photos, video clips, or images you create or find

Design backgrounds or add visual effects if needed

Arrange the sequence so the story feels real and emotional

Add music and simple effects to make the video polished and professional



The first version doesn’t have to be perfect, but it should be very close to perfect (around 80%). I will then give feedback for final tweaks.



Requirements

Proven experience creating similar videos (MUST show samples)

Creative and able to tell a story with visuals

Knowledge of tools such as:

DaVinci Resolve

Adobe Premiere Pro

Adobe After Effects

Mind Movies (plus)

Able to follow instructions carefully and understand the concept

Professional and serious about delivering high-quality work

IMPORTANT – PROJECT GROUP + PAID SAMPLE



MPORTANT – THIS IS NOT JUST A VIDEO EDITING JOB



This is NOT a basic editing job where you are only cutting clips together.



You will be responsible for creating full scenes from scratch, not just editing existing footage.



This includes:



Creating or sourcing realistic backgrounds (parks, restaurants, event spaces, etc.)

Creating or selecting realistic people/characters to fit the scenes

Combining clips, images, or assets in a way that makes the story feel real

Using creativity to build 
nts that may not exist in raw footage



I may provide some clips of myself, but you will need to integrate them into fully created scenes so it feels like everything is happening naturally in real life.



You must be comfortable using:



Stock footage

Creative compositing

Visual storytelling techniques



If you are only experienced in basic video editing, this role is not a good fit.



I will be creating a group where I will share the full project details.



Participants in the group will be asked to complete a sample task, which WILL BE PAID according to OnlineJobs.ph rules.

After getting the best sample I will choose that person to do the actual project (and hopefully other projects in the future as well)



This is required before final selection.



How to Apply

join whatsapp group





Send a voice note or text on WhatsApp:


Include:



Explanation of how you understand the project

How you would approach it

Samples of similar videos you have made



Only apply if you can deliver a high-quality video close to perfect on the first try.



I do NOT have time for trial-and-error (less than 80%) or experimental work. 

You need to have real experience and confidence in what you can create.



Pay



$50–$100 total (depending on experience and portfolio)



Payment options:



Down payment via PayPal, OR

Submit first version with watermark until full payment



Other Notes



The video must feel real, emotional, and immersive

You must use your creativity to build scenes, backgrounds, and flow

I am looking for someone professional, skilled, and creative

The video should be mostly correct on the first try



This is a manifestation/visualization project, so it is very important that the story feels believable and emotionally engaging.





Want early access to new job opportunities before they are posted here?

title: Assistant Controller & Financial Operations
description:
We are seeking a Senior-level, high-autonomy Assistant Controller to join our leadership support team. This is not a training position; we are looking for a seasoned professional who can step in on day one with the "clinical precision" required for a Healthcare Provider partner. You will be expected to bridge the gap between daily bookkeeping and high-level financial reporting without oversight, maintaining the "Nodal Management" of our fiscal operations with military-grade accuracy.





Key Responsibilities



Financial Control & Reporting

- Expert Reconciliation: Perform and oversee regular reconciliation of all financial accounts, ensuring 100?ta integrity.



- Executive Reporting: Prepare weekly, monthly, and year-end financial reports (P&L, cash flow, balance sheets) for the CEO and CRO.



- Audit & Tax Leadership: Independently coordinate with the company’s CPA for quarterly/year-end tax filings and complex payroll reports.



- Strategic Expense Management: Proactively track company expenses and identify variances or areas for fiscal improvement.



- Accounts Management: Oversight of AP/AR, ensuring vendor payments and invoice follow-ups are handled without internal reminders.



Payroll & Contractor Management

- Hybrid Payroll Mastery: Independently process and submit payroll for internal staff and independent contractors with 100?curacy.



- Advanced Deductions: Manage intricate payroll and contractor deductions, specifically for company-covered vehicle and fuel expenses.



- Rapid Dispute Resolution: Resolve driver pay discrepancies within 24 hours.



Insurance & Fleet Administration

- Policy Oversight: Manage Auto Insurance (adding/removing Vehicles), General Liability (GL/PL), and Workers' Comp policies.



- Compliance Lead: Track i
 reports and lead coordination with insurance brokers for annual renewals and audits.



HR & Compliance Support

- Rapid Onboarding: Execute new hire paperwork, background checks, and tax forms (W-9) within a strict 3-day window.



- Data Integrity: Maintain absolute accuracy in employee and contractor records.



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------



Required Qualifications

- Senior Experience: 5–7+ years of verifiable senior bookkeeping and payroll experience. We are seeking an expert, not a candidate who requires fundamental training.



- Software Mastery: Expert-level proficiency in QuickBooks Online (Required) and advanced Excel (VLOOKUPs, Pivot Tables).



- Professional Depth: Strong mastery of accounts payable/receivable and extensive experience working directly with external CPAs.



- Industry Context: Prior experience in NEMT, logistics, or healthcare documentation/DOT requirements is a significant advantage.



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------



 Performance Metrics (KPIs)

- Precision: 100% payroll accuracy and on-time processing each pay period.



- Speed: Monthly reconciliations completed within 5 business days of month-end.



- Reliability: 95% of insurance policy tasks and 100% of vendor bills handled on or before the deadline.



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------



A Note for Applicants

We are an institutional-grade organization that values structure, follow-through, and getting the details right the first time. If you require significant guidance on basic accounting principles or software navigation, this role is not the right fit. We are looking for a financial partner to help scale our operations.

title: Business Development Representative (Remote) – Staffing Agency
description:
About Us



We are an established staffing agency based in California, USA. But what exactly is a staffing agency? Think of us as the middleman between companies that need workers and people who are looking for jobs. Businesses come to us when they need to hire — instead of spending their own time searching for candidates, we do the work for them. We find, screen, and place the right people into the right jobs. Simple as that!



We work with both blue-collar jobs (such as warehouse workers, drivers, construction, manufacturing, etc.) and white-collar jobs (such as office administrators, accountants, managers, HR professionals, etc.). We are proud to staff workers nationwide across the entire United States.





The Opportunity



We are growing and looking to bring on a motivated, self-driven Business Development Representative to help us connect with more client companies across the country. This is a remote position and we are sourcing candidates through this platform.





What You Will Be Doing



Your main goal is simple — find and bring in new client companies that need staffing services.



You will do this by:





    ???? Sending
s to potential client companies



    ???? Making cold calls to businesses that may need staffing solutions



    ???? Building relationships and convincing companies to sign up for our staffing services







How This Works – Two Stages



? Stage 1 – Getting Started (Commission-Based)





    This role starts as commission-based only



    You will earn a commission for every new client company you successfully bring in



    You have the freedom to work your own schedule and hours



    However, we strongly prefer that you are available during our business hours:

    Monday – Friday | 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Pacific Time / California)

    (This is important due to time zone differences and so we can communicate and support you when needed)







? Stage 2 – Growth & Stability (Salary + Commission)





    Once you have proven results — meaning the companies you bring in are:



        ?? Signed up for our services



        ?? Actively requesting workers



        ?? Being invoiced by us







    We will negotiate a competitive hourly rate/salary on top of your commission



    You will continue to earn commission on any new business you bring in







What We Are Looking For





    Someone self-motivated and results-driven



    Comfortable with cold calling and
 outreach



    Strong communication skills — written and verbal



    Ability to work independently without constant supervision



    Sales or business development experience is a plus but not required



    Must be able to reach out to businesses across the USA







What We Offer





    ???? Commission earnings from day one



    ???? Growth potential into a salaried + commission role



    ???? Flexible working hours



    ???? A supportive team and long-term partnership opportunity



    ???? 100% Remote — work from anywhere







Our Vision



We are not just looking for someone to fill a role — we are looking for a long-term partner who grows with us. As our business grows, so does your earning potential. We value loyalty, hard work, and results — and we reward them.







    ???? Interested? Apply now and let's start building something great together!

    We look forward to hearing from motivated individuals who are ready to hit the ground running.

title: Video & Podcast Editor (Remote, Per-Project to Full-Time)
description:
Copy



ABOUT US

 

We are a Canadian outdoor media company that produces multiple podcasts, YouTube channels, online courses, and social media content. Our content covers firearms safety training, hunting, fishing, precision shooting, backcountry skills, and wild food. We produce content across two brands and are looking for a dedicated editor to handle a growing volume of post-production work.

 

This is not a one-off gig. We are looking for someone who starts on a per-project basis and grows into a full-time role as we build trust and volume together.

 

 

WHAT YOU WILL BE EDITING (STARTING SCOPE)

 

You will begin with the following types of work:

 

- Short-form social clips (Reels, Shorts, TikToks) extracted from longer podcast and video content, with captions and vertical formatting. You will use Opus Clip to identify clip candidates, then review, refine, and finish them to a publishable standard.

 

- YouTube travel and adventure videos. We produce content from production trips (for example, a recent trip to Sweden that included factory tours, hunting content, and a training academy). You will receive raw footage and B-roll and edit it into polished standalone videos.

 

- Cooking, wild food, and outdoor lifestyle content for social media, blog posts, and promotional use. Short, clean, visually engaging edits.

 

- Online course video modules. Screen recordings with voiceover, slide-based instructional content, and training walkthroughs. Clean editing, clear audio, and professional presentation.

 

As you learn our style and demonstrate reliability, the scope will expand to include full podcast episode editing across multiple shows (60-90+ minute episodes, audio and video formats, including remote guest audio repair).

 

 

TYPICAL WEEKLY VOLUME (STARTING)

 

- 5-10+ short-form social clips

- 1-2 YouTube videos (complexity varies)

- Course content as needed

- Cooking/outdoor lifestyle edits as needed

 

This will grow. The right person will be handling significantly more volume within 60-90 days.

 

 

WHAT THIS ROLE REQUIRES BEYOND BASIC EDITING

 

We need more than someone who can make clean cuts. You will also:

 

- Review raw footage and organize it, identifying the strongest content and flagging what works best for a given video. Some shoots produce hours of footage that need to be reviewed and distilled into the best possible output.

 

- Source music and sound effects from 
 (our licensing platform). You need to find tracks that fit the tone and pacing of each piece.

 

- Apply color correction and grading appropriate to the content. Outdoor field footage requires different treatment than studio or screen-based content.

 

- Create and maintain subtitle/caption files (SRT and burned-in) for all video content.

 

- Create thumbnails for YouTube and social platforms.

 

- Over time, contribute to pre-production planning by telling us what footage and shot types you need to do your job well (establishing shots, close-ups, cutaways, B-roll). We want to build a feedback loop where the shooting and editing process improves together.

 

 

TOOLS AND WORKFLOW

 

You should be proficient in or willing to quickly learn:

 

Editing: Adobe Premiere Pro (primary), DaVinci Resolve (used for some projects and color work)

Audio: iZotope RX or equivalent for noise reduction, leveling, de-essing, and spectral editing

Workflow: Opus Clip (we will provide access for identifying social clip candidates from long-form content), 
 (music and SFX licensing)

Delivery: Google Drive or Dropbox for file delivery, Slack or
 for daily communication

Other: Photoshop or Canva for thumbnails, subtitle/caption tools

 

You do not need to know all of these on day one, but you need to be comfortable learning new tools quickly.

 

 

WHAT WE EXPECT (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

 

Read this section carefully.

 

- You must review the ENTIRE file before making edits. Previous editors we have worked with edited the first 10 minutes and ignored problems in the rest. This is an automatic disqualification.

 

- Turnaround times must be met consistently. Late delivery without communication is grounds for ending the relationship.

 

- You must communicate proactively. If something is unclear, ask. If you are going to miss a deadline, tell us before the deadline. If you need something from us (footage, a file, a decision), ask for it immediately.

 

- You must be willing to receive direct feedback and adjust your work without taking it personally. We will tell you exactly what needs to change and we expect it to change.

 

- You are expected to improve over time. As you learn our content and style, the quality and speed of your work should increase.

 

 

LEARNING OUR STYLE

 

Our content has a specific tone and feel. It is direct, competent, and serious without being stiff. It is not flashy, over-produced, or trendy. You will spend your first few weeks studying our published content to internalize the style. We will provide a style guide, but the real learning comes from watching and listening to what we have already put out.

 

You do not need to know anything about firearms, hunting, or outdoor skills to start. But you need to be willing to learn enough to make intelligent editing decisions.

 

 

COMPENSATION

 

This role starts on a per-project basis:

 

- Short-form social clip (vertical, captioned, 30-60 sec): $5-10 USD per clip

- YouTube video edit (5-10 min, cuts, color, captions, B-roll, music): $30-60 USD depending on complexity

- Online course module edit (5-10 min, screen recording/slides, audio cleanup): $20-40 USD depending on length

- Podcast episode (60-90 min, full edit, audio cleanup, export) when scope expands: $50-75 USD per episode

 

Payment monthly via Wise, PayPal, or direct deposit for all completed work.

 

For the right person, this role will transition to a full-time monthly retainer within 60-90 days. We are not looking for the cheapest option. We are looking for someone reliable, detail-oriented, and committed to long-term work.

 

 

HOW TO APPLY

 

Send a message that includes:

 

1. A link to your portfolio or 3 examples of edited videos or podcasts (YouTube links preferred). At least one example must show your ability to create engaging short-form social content.

2. A brief description of your editing setup (computer specs, software you use, internet speed).

3. Your availability (hours per day and timezone).

4. Your expected per-project rates (or confirm you are comfortable with the rates listed above).

5. Your experience with any of the following: Opus Clip, 
, iZotope RX, DaVinci Resolve color grading, AutoPod. If none, tell us what you do use.

6. One sentence explaining why you are a good fit for long-term work. Not a generic cover letter.

 

Applications that do not include all six items will not be reviewed.

title: Real Estate Acquisitions Specialist / Closer (Remote | Part-Time | Texas Market)
description:
We are looking for an experienced Real Estate Acquisitions Specialist / Closer to join a growing wholesale and fix-and-flip operation based in Texas.



This is a remote, part-time role with a strong commission structure for high performers.



ROLE OVERVIEW

You will be responsible for speaking with property owners, building rapport, negotiating deals, and helping move properties into contract. This is a sales-driven acquisitions role, not an entry-level position.



Training will be provided on systems and process, but you must already be confident in real estate closing and acquisitions conversations.



SCHEDULE

Monday – Friday

8:00 AM – 1:00 PM CST

(Part-time remote)



COMPENSATION

$10 – $12 per hour

PLUS commissions:



$300 per closed deal (first 3 deals)

$500 per closed deal (starting 4th deal onward)

title: creative short form editor
description:
I’m looking for a high-level short-form editor who can turn travel footage into viral, high-retention content for TikTok, Instagram Reels,
, and YouTube Shorts.



This is NOT a basic clipping role.



I already run a travel channel: Arthur Hill Travels (YouTube). I post long-form travel content exploring alternative and lesser-known destinations.



What I’m looking for:



I want someone who can re-imagine and restructure long-form travel content into short-form narratives that feel fresh, funny, and highly engaging.



Not just cutting down videos, but:



Rebuilding the story from scratch

Changing the narrative angle

Turning 
nts into 
s or comedic beats

Creating curiosity-driven storytelling

Maximising retention and rewatchability



Inspiration / style reference:



Harry Jaggard, Luke Damont, Seal On Tour. 



These creators don’t just clip long videos — they reframe them into completely different story experiences using humour, pacing changes, 
 culture, narrative restructuring, and strong hooks.



Key skills required:



Turn long-form travel videos into viral short-form content

Understand internet culture / 
 humour

Create strong hooks in the first 1–2 seconds if needed

Reorder footage to build a new narrative (not just summaries)

Edit for retention, pacing, and rewatchability

Think creatively, not mechanically



This is ideal for 
 editors, story-driven short-form editors, and viral TikTok / Reels editors.



Platforms content will be used for:

TikTok, Instagram Reels,
 Reels, YouTube Shorts.



Content should be targeted at Western audiences (US / Europe), but any virality is good virality. 



Trial task (IMPORTANT):



If you’re interested, please complete this test:



Go to my YouTube channel: Arthur Hill Travels

Choose any long-form travel video

Create a short-form video using the footage

You can restructure it completely

You can change the narrative angle

You can use 
 culture / humour / storytelling

You can ask me to do a voiceover or video if you provide me with a script 

DO NOT just shorten it or summarize it

I want creativity and originality



Send your submission to:





I will choose the best one.



Payment structure:



I’m offering a performance-based revenue share model:



You receive 100% of revenue generated by the shorts

Plus an additional 40% bonus on top



So effectively: 140% payout based on content performance.



You can create as many or as few reels as you want, as long as they are high quality and suitable for posting across my accounts. There is no fixed output requirement — your earnings scale with both creativity and consistency, so the more strong content you produce, the more you can earn.



In some cases I may request one round of revisions just to ensure the content aligns with the tone and direction of the channel.

title: Special Project Coordinator
description:
Hours: 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM CST (Central Time, US) — non-negotiable

Time tracking requirement: Use of Hubstaff is mandatory for time and activity tracking.



About Us

We are Payment Studio — a payment-services organisation with a main company and a growing portfolio of sub-brands/ventures. We regularly create new brands and will rely on you to help launch, onboard and promote them. We also engage in trade shows and industry events related to payments and other special projects as needed. You will play a central role in these brand launches and event efforts.



Role Summary

As our Special Project Coordinator, you will:



- Lead the onboarding and launch of new sub-companies/brands: setting up processes, coordinating stakeholders, executing timelines and ensuring a smooth start-up for each new entity.

- Research, organise and coordinate visits to trade shows, conferences and other events relevant to payments / fintech / commerce—handling logistics, scheduling, vendor/vendor-exhibitor relations, and follow-up.

- Maintain project documentation, status updates, trackers, tasks, reminders and dependencies across all special projects.

- Work closely with cross-functional teams (marketing, operations, finance, partnerships) to ensure all necessary steps are completed for each brand launch and event.

- Proactively identify project risks, issues or delays and escalate / remediate them.

- Handle “other special projects as they come” — since our business moves quickly and new initiatives will arise, you’ll need flexibility, adaptability and ownership.



Key Requirements



- Able to work full-time during US CST business hours (9 AM–6 PM CST) Monday–Friday.

- Strong project coordination/management experience, preferably with brand launches, onboarding of companies or event/trade-show coordination.

- Excellent English communication (written and verbal) — you will be interacting with global stakeholders.

- Excellent organisational skills, ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously, set priorities, follow timelines and deliver on schedule.

- Familiarity with project-tracking tools, spreadsheets, task trackers, status reports. Experience with
-tracking tools is a plus.

- Must be able to use Hubstaff for
 tracking.

- Comfortable working remotely and self-driving your work, with clear communication of status and blockers.

- Proactive, detail-oriented, adaptable, and ready for the fast-paced nature of launching new brands and supporting event logistics.



How to Apply

Please apply via the link below:

description:

We’re seeking a highly organized, proactive, and professional Executive Assistant to provide remote support to two executives at our firm. This role is ideal for someone with strong communication skills and prior executive support experience who can manage priorities, anticipate needs, and keep day-to-day operations running smoothly.

mbers

- Ability to prioritize tasks and manage multiple responsibilities

- Proactive, resourceful, and detail-oriented by adding TAX to your subject line to show that skill.

Requirements

s, funnels, and assets.

- Able to differentiate AI writing from human writing (tone, rhythm, specificity, emotional truth, and “over-polished” patterns).

- Extremely detail-oriented: grammar, formatting, brand consistency, and follow-through.



Requirements:

- Genuinely loves organizing

- Proven experience with Simplero (or equivalent platform expertise + willingness to become a Simplero power user fast).

- Strong project management and “make it happen” execution.

- Comfortable juggling admin + creative production without dropping details.

- Wordpress VA

- Experience supporting authors, coaches, or spiritual/wellness brands.

title: Accounting  /  Tax Assistant
description:
Role Overview

If you enjoy being the person who keeps everything organized, on track, and running smoothly—and want to play a critical role in delivering a high-end client experience—this role is for you.



DiLucci CPA Firm has been serving business owners and real estate investors since 2005. For nearly two decades, we’ve built a reputation for doing tax differently—by going deeper than compliance, telling clients the truth, and using the tax code strategically to protect and grow their wealth. Our mission is simple: help clients keep more of what they earn while giving them clarity, confidence, and a long-term plan. We hold ourselves to a high bar: Genuine Care, Great Communication, Speed to Learning, and Fun Tax Energy.



As an Accounting/Tax Assistant, you will serve as the operational backbone of the tax planning team—owning scheduling, client communication, document collection, and workflow tracking. You are the person ensuring nothing falls through the cracks, clients feel supported, and the team operates at a high level.



This is a fast-paced, high-ownership role ideal for someone who is proactive, detail-oriented, and thrives in keeping multiple moving pieces aligned.



Key Responsibilities



Scheduling & Client Coordination

- Manage and coordinate client scheduling across the tax planning team

- Ensure calendars are optimized, appointments are confirmed, and meetings are fully prepared

- Serve as a point of contact for clients to ensure a smooth, professional experience



Document Collection & Follow-Ups

- Proactively request, track, and follow up on missing client documents

- Ensure all required information is received ahead of planning deadlines

- Maintain accountability with clients while delivering a high-touch experience



File Organization & Documentation

- Organize and maintain clean, structured client files within internal systems

- Ensure all documents are properly labeled, uploaded, and accessible

- Keep client information centralized and easy for the team to navigate



Discovery Notes & Preparation

- Create clear, organized discovery notes from client data and internal inputs

- Prepare summaries ahead of planning calls so advisors are fully equipped

- Ensure accuracy and completeness of all pre-call materials



Workflow Tracking & Execution

- Track the status of all active clients and ensure work is progressing on time

- Identify bottlenecks and take action to keep projects moving forward

- Coordinate across tea
mbers during high-volume periods



Client Communication & Support

- Manage ongoing client communication through
 and internal systems

- Respond promptly and professionally to client inquiries

- Ensure all client touchpoints are handled with care and no communication goes unanswered



Role Expectations

- Maintain strong organization across multiple clients, deadlines, and workflows

- Ensure timely follow-ups and consistent client communication

- Operate proactively by identifying issues before they become problems

- Keep internal systems clean, accurate, and up to date

- Be a reliable, steady presence that the team can depend on

- To qualify, write "Tax" in the Middle of your Subject Line and at the end of your message.



Requirements

- At least 1 year experience in US Accounting or Taxation

- Quickbooks/Xero experience

- Strong organizational and time management skills

- Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities simultaneously

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills (high English proficiency)

- High attention to detail and strong follow-through

- Ability to work in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment

- Comfortable using digital tools and systems for tracking and communication



Benefits & Perks

- Opportunity to grow within a fast-scaling, high-performance team

R. Riveter · United States-based brand · American-assembled handbags supporting military spouses

Part to full time / Contract · Remote



This is not a "post content" role. We're looking for someone who can translate a brand's mission into content that builds community, drives growth, and shows up for our customers.



About R. Riveter

R. Riveter is a mission-driven American brand that creates flexible employment for military spouses through distributed manufacturing. Our brand is built on storytelling, heritage, and community — every product has a purpose and a person behind it.



What you'll do



Plan and manage a content calendar 4 weeks in advance

Create and schedule posts across Instagram,
, and TikTok using a scheduling app

Produce Reels, carousels, short-form video, and static graphics

Monitor and respond to comments, DMs, and mentions across all platforms — engaging authentically with our community in our brand voice

Stay current on platform trends, algorithm changes, and content formats

Collaborate with our team to ensure all content reflects brand voice and mission





Requirements



Proven experience managing social media for a brand or business (portfolio required)

Proficient in Canva, Adobe, or equivalent graphic design tools

Experience editing short-form video (Reels / TikTok style)

Strong written English — able to write in brand voice, not just captions

Experience with customer-facing community engagement — responding to comments and DMs professionally and warmly

Self-managed, proactive, and organized — you plan ahead, not react

Genuine interest in mission-driven brands and storytelling

Must be able to work EST overlap by 5 hours in hours 8AM-5PM EST





How to apply

We don't just want a resume. We want to see how you think. Your application must include both of the following:

Part 1 — Brand audit

Review our current social media presence on Instagram,
, and 
 tell us:



What you would keep

What you would change

What you would add



The more specific and thoughtful your audit, the more likely you are to get an interview.

Part 2 — Content exercise

Using this blog post as your source material:

From the Flight Line to Forever ? 

Create a high-performing Instagram content concept in one or more of these formats: Reel, carousel, short-form video, or static graphic.

Your submission must include:



3 hook options — first 3 seconds (Reel) or first slide (Carousel)

Visual cues — specific imagery, text overlays, and movement for each section

Content flow — how the story unfolds, concisely

CTA — must feel natural and on-brand; "link in bio" is not acceptable





To apply, submit:

Your resume or work history + completed brand audit + completed content exercise.

Applications without both exercises will not be reviewed. We recognize this is a more involved application than most — and that's intentional. We're using your audit and content concept to identify applicants who can hit the ground running with our brand. If you can do the work, show us.

title: Bookkeeper – AppFolio & QuickBooks Operator
description:
We are not looking for a general bookkeeper.



We are hiring someone who has already been the primary bookkeeper for a property management company managing multiple owners/clients (not just one property owner that self manages)—and can step in and run this function without training.



**Non-negotiables (do not apply if these don’t apply to you):**



* Direct, hands-on experience using AppFolio in a property management company

* Proven experience as the primary bookkeeper for a company managing properties for multiple clients (not just personal portfolios)

* Strong working knowledge of QuickBooks, including setting up and back-loading properties and financials from prior years

* Formal education in bookkeeping or accounting (degree required)



**What you bring to the table:**



* Deep understanding of property management fees, owner distributions, trust accounting, and multi-owner structures

* Ability to accurately transfer and reconcile AppFolio reporting into QuickBooks

* Confidence owning the entire bookkeeping function with minimal oversight



**What success looks like:**



* Clean, accurate books across multiple properties and owners

* Trust accounts handled correctly and confidently

* Seamless systems between AppFolio and QuickBooks

* You take ownership and elevate the role—we don’t manage you



**Important:**

This is not a training role. We are looking for an expert who can hit the ground running and show us how this function should operate.



HOW TO APPLY:

1. A 1-minute voice sample (Vocaroo is an option to submit this)

2. Your WPM score with the 3 Minute Test: 
/student/tests

3. Your experience using AppFolio in a property management company

4. How you’ve handled syncing/reconciling AppFolio with QuickBooks

5. Your experience setting up or back-loading properties and financials



*Applications missing any of the above will not be reviewed.

*Generic applications will not be considered.

title: Full-Time Video Producer/Editor for True Crime YouTube Channel
description:
We are launching a new long-form true crime YouTube channel focused on real footage storytelling — bodycam, interrogation, courtroom, 911 audio. Think JCS - Criminal Psychology meets Crime Explorer. We are looking for a full-time Producer/Editor to be our creative partner from day one.



This is not a traditional editor role. You will research cases, draft narrative outlines, write first-draft scripts, and edit the final video. You are responsible for storytelling, not just cuts.



ABOUT THE CHANNEL:



- Format: Long-form documentary-style episodes built around real, public footage

- Length: Variable — story dictates length (some 15 min, some 45+ min)

- Tone: Analytical, evidence-driven, respectful. Quality over quantity.

- Target output: 2 videos per week



WHAT YOU WILL DO EACH WEEK:



- Research the assigned case using public sources (court records, news archives, prior coverage)

- Draft a narrative outline and first-draft narration script

- Cut and assemble the video using real footage as the visual backbone

- Burn subtitles, mix audio, deliver thumbnail concepts

- Take editorial notes fro
 and revise until the work is approved



SCHEDULE AND PAY:



- Full-time: 40 hours per week, Monday-Friday, 8-hour shifts

- Schedule must overlap with US Central time at least 4 hours per day

- Starting pay: $1,500-1,800/month USD depending on experience, paid via Wise

- 13th-month bonus included

- 2 weeks paid time off per year

- Performance reviews quarterly with raise potential tied to channel growth



HOW THE WORK FLOWS:



- Each Monday (your time), we sync briefly via
 to confirm the week's deliverable

- You receive: case brief, raw footage in a shared folder, target deliverable

- Daily brief check-ins via
 (5 minutes — what you worked on, what's next, any blockers)

- One Google Meet video call per week to review work and align on direction

- Friday wrap with a quick written review of what shipped



You will work standard 8-hour shifts. Pay is fixed. Target output is 2 videos per week — we will work together to refine the workflow until that pace is sustainable.



REQUIRED SKILLS:



- Strong command of written and spoken English. You will write scripts and join weekly video calls. Both must be confident and clear.

- High-speed home internet: 50+ Mbps download and 10+ Mbps upload. Stable connection. We will be transferring large video files daily.

- Willingness to interview over Google Meet during the screening process

- Willingness to attend a weekly Google Meet check-in once hired

- Proven track record editing long-form YouTube content for an established channel (please link to 2-3 examples in your application)

- Comfortable with footage-driven, narration-light formats (not stock-photo-heavy)

- Skilled with Adobe Premiere, DaVinci Resolve, or Final Cut Pro

- Subtitle work, audio mixing, basic thumbnail design

- Reliable power (or backup plan)



STRONGLY PREFERRED:



- Familiarity with the true crime genre

- Background or interest in journalism, documentary, or narrative storytelling

- Experience working with US-based clients across time zones



NOT REQUIRED:



- Voice acting or narration (we are hiring a separate voice actor)

- On-camera presence (this is a faceless channel)



SCREENING PROCESS:



1. Submit your application with: (a) links to 2-3 long-form videos you have edited, (b) brief description of your role on each, (c) your current availability, (d) your home internet speed (run a speedtest at 
 or speedtest.net and include the result)

2. Top candidates will receive a screening test: edit a 5-minute opening segment from raw footage we provide, with a 3-paragraph rationale explaining your editorial choices. 3-day turnaround.

3. Finalists will be invited to a 30-minute Google Meet interview

4. Top finisher receives a one-week paid trial at full rate

5. After successful trial, full-time engagement begins



WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT:



- You are not a piecework editor. You are a creative partner with strategic input.

- Long-term stable employment with quarterly raises tied to performance, not gig work

- We have the budget and infrastructure to do this properly. We are not pinching pennies and asking for unrealistic output.



TO APPLY:



Send your application with portfolio links, your speedtest result, and answer this short question in your response:



"Tell me about a video you edited where the story didn't work, and what you would change about it now."



We are filtering for storytellers, not technicians. Generic applications without portfolio links and speedtest results will not be reviewed. Looking forward to working with the right person.

title: Technical Sales Engineer - Laboratory Materials and Equipment
description:
MSE Supplies LLC is seeking a highly driven Technical Sales Specialist to lead and expand our Materials Science and Life Sciences teams. This role will represent consumables such as Engineered Ceramics, Crystals and general lab supplies including but not limited to; tissue culture, liquid handling, cold storage, blood collection, microscopy, histology, PCR/qPCR and others. In addition, the product portfolio will offer a full capital expenditure equipment portfolio including arc melters, melt spinners, coating equipment & PVD systems. 



This role is designed for a business-oriented professional who is passionate about bridging scientific innovation with commercial success and working in fast moving team environment. As a key member of the sales team, you will influence the direction of our product strategy, drive sales growth, and support global customers in achieving breakthrough research outcomes and be well compensated for all responsibilities. 



Responsibilities

•	Execute strategic territory and sales plans, actively managing a robust pipeline and maintain accurate opportunity tracking within CRM systems to support forecasting and consistently achieve or exceed revenue targets 

•	Own commercial performance for a defined portfolio of laboratory products, driving revenue growth, margin expansion, and market penetration 

•	Serve as the technical sales expert for assigned product lines (e.g., advanced materials, chemicals, lab equipment, instrumentation), aligning solutions to customer applications 

•	Drive consultative, solution-based sales, positioning MSE Supplies as a preferred partner for both standard and customized solutions 

•	Develop and expand key customer relationships, engaging decision-makers to identify opportunities, negotiate agreements, and grow share of wallet 

•	Proactively manage inbound and outbound sales opportunities, converting qualified leads into revenue through structured follow-up and closing strategies 

•	Deliver high-impact technical presentations, product demonstrations, and application-driven discussions to accelerate customer decision-making 

•	Provide pre-sales technical support and commercial guidance, ensuring alignment between customer needs and product capabilities to drive conversion and retention 

•	Collaborate cross-functionally with Sales, Marketing, Product Management, and Operations to align go-to-market strategy and execution 

•	Act as the voice of the customer, capturing market insights, competitive intelligence, and unmet needs to inform product development and commercial strategy 



Qualifications and Skills



•	Bachelor’s degree in Chemistry, Materials Science, Biology, Chemical Engineering, or a related technical field required; equivalent industry experience with a proven track record of sales success in scientific markets may be considered in lieu of a degree

•	3+ years of experience in technical sales, applications, or customer-facing roles within scientific markets 

•	Demonstrated success in achieving sales targets and managing a pipeline within life sciences, advanced materials, semiconductor, energy, or industrial R&D markets 

•	Strong understanding of laboratory products, workflows, and customer applications 

•	Proven ability to translate technical expertise into commercial value through consultative selling 

•	Experience using CRM platforms (e.g., Salesforce) for pipeline management, forecasting, and activity tracking 

•	Excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills with the ability to engage both technical and business stakeholders 

•	Strong organizational and territory management skills, with the ability to manage multiple opportunities simultaneously 

•	Results-driven, self-motivated, and capable of operating effectively in a field-based or hybrid sales environment 



Benefits:

• PTO (Paid Time Off) & Sick Time



Come join us to enable innovations for scientists and engineers worldwide. In this position, you will play a key role in working directly with our large customers base and helping customers purchase the products they need to Enable Innovation. MSE Supplies LLC has built a winning track record of ~100% annual growth rate over the past 5 years. Our company is on a path to accelerated growth. Along the way we will all have a lot of fun celebrating successes together. Come join us!



Company Overview



Trusted by research labs and innovative companies worldwide, MSE Supplies LLC is a U.S.-based leading supplier of high-quality materials, lab equipment, materials characterization analytical services and customized solutions for both research and production. With a global customer base of more than 2,000 research universities, government labs, as well as Fortune 500 companies and technology start-ups, we support scientists and engineers worldwide to accelerate their innovations. As a fast-growing company, we are constantly looking for talented and ambitious people to join our team and develop your successful career.



Our Products and Services (Both Standard and Customized)

• (A to Z) Single Crystals, Wafers and Substrates

• Arc Melting, Melt Spinning and Quenching Equipment (made in Germany by Edmund Buhler)

• Ball Milling Equipment and Accessories (mills, jars, grinding balls, milling media and microbeads)

• Biotech Lab Supplies

• Conductive Oxide (FTO, ITO, AZO) Substrates

• Customized Sputtering Targets

• FAST / SPS / DCS Sintering Presses (made in Germany by Dr. Fritsch)

• Gallium Nitride (GaN) and Silicon Carbide (SiC) compound semiconductor substrates and wafers

• High Purity Inorganic Chemicals

• High Purity Lab Crucibles

title: Technical Sales Engineer - Laboratory Materials and Equipment
description:
Build your future within ours



MSE Supplies LLC has a career opportunity for a Technical Sales Engineer - Lab Products. Come join us to enable innovations for scientists and engineers worldwide. In this position, you will play a key role in developing our lab products business portfolio and altogether the direction of our company. MSE Supplies LLC has built a winning track record of ~100% annual growth rate over the past 5 years. Our company is on a path to accelerated growth. Along the way we will all have a lot of fun celebrating successes together. Come join us!



As Technical Sales Engineer you’ll work with the Customer Service Team as well as Business Development & Product Management Teams to create, qualify and confirm sales opportunities. This is a key role as you will be working closely with customers providing support and guidance with their purchases. Strong customer service skills, sales knowledge and sales administration experience is required to be fully effective.



There is also opportunity for advancement and growth in the company.



Responsibilities will include:

• Provides excellent customer service through respectful and courteous address of questions regarding services, products,

and invoices.

• Basic computer ease, comfort, and fluency or the ability to quickly gain necessary skills.

• Can demonstrate exceptional verbal and non-verbal communication skills.

• Research new accounts to identify new opportunities.

• 2yrs of insides sales or customer service experience is preferred.

• Communicating with customers with technical product knowledge to promote the sales of lab products offered by MSE

Supplies

• Providing customer support before, during and after sales



Requirements:

• Minimum a college degree in a STEM major is required. Materials Science and Engineering or Chemistry or Biochemistry

majors preferred.

• 1+ years laboratory R&D experience preferred

• Must be able to work on PST time zone, Monday - Friday

• Experience in lab supplies and lab equipment for chemistry and materials science research or biotech R&D preferred

• Strong communication skills

• Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a remote environment

• Strong ability to address multiple tasks simultaneously to manage projects effectively

• Organized and efficient. Details-oriented & ability to work under time constraints

• Strong interest in technology and willingness to learn about products and customer needs

• Self-motivated and trustworthy



Benefits:

• PTO (Paid Time Off) & Sick Time



Come join us to enable innovations for scientists and engineers worldwide. In this position, you will play a key role in working directly with our large customers base and helping customers purchase the products they need to Enable Innovation. MSE Supplies LLC has built a winning track record of ~100% annual growth rate over the past 5 years. Our company is on a path to accelerated growth. Along the way we will all have a lot of fun celebrating successes together. Come join us!



Company Overview



Trusted by research labs and innovative companies worldwide, MSE Supplies LLC is a U.S.-based leading supplier of high-quality materials, lab equipment, materials characterization analytical services and customized solutions for both research and production. With a global customer base of more than 2,000 research universities, government labs, as well as Fortune 500 companies and technology start-ups, we support scientists and engineers worldwide to accelerate their innovations. As a fast-growing company, we are constantly looking for talented and ambitious people to join our team and develop your successful career.



Our Products and Services (Both Standard and Customized)

• (A to Z) Single Crystals, Wafers and Substrates

• High Purity Inorganic Chemicals

• High Purity Lab Crucibles

• Lithium Battery Materials and Tools (solid electrolytes, anodes, cathodes, battery research consumables and tools)

• Materials Characterization Analytical Services

• Metal Powders for Additive Manufacturing (3D printing)

• Monolayer Graphene Films and Graphene Oxide

title: Senior SEO + AI Operations Lead — WordPress + Local SEO — Long-Term Full-Time
description:
We're a new US Rank & Rent operation building a portfolio of 100+ local-service WordPress websites. We're looking for a senior SEO + AI operator who can own day-to-day production, lead a small team, and grow with us for years.



This is not a junior role. This is not a content-writer role. We need someone who has built, ranked, and managed dozens of WordPress sites before, who thinks in systems, and who treats SEO like a craft.



ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll run our entire AI + SEO production line from end to end.



We are finding the best markets to allow our new sites to rank on page 1 and get leads easily and fast. We can be in any niche and any city we want, which ever is the easiest to rank fast. Our goal is scale. To have as many sites as possible. These are basic sites with basic SEO. You need to be an expert at setting up new sites and getting them to rank on page 1 fast in these low competition markets and then we duplicate this again and again. 



AI Tools below are subject to change and we appreciate what you prefer to use as the expert******



AI website generation (10Web AI Builder, ZipWP)

AI content stack (Koala Writer, Penfriend.ai, Frase, SEOWriting.ai)

AI receptionist setup (Goodcall + Twilio per site)

On-page SEO (Rank Math Pro with AI features, schema, internal linking via LinkWhisper)

Local SEO (BrightLocal citations, NAP consistency, local rank tracking)

Quality control (you own the 12-point QC checklist for every site we publish)

Reporting (daily Slack updates, weekly Notion reports, monthly KPI review)



You'll report to the owner via a 30-minute weekly Zoom plus async daily Slack and Loom updates.

WHAT A TYPICAL WEEK LOOKS LIKE



Monday: review the owner's approved keyword/city queue (8-10 new sites/week at peak velocity)

Tuesday-Thursday: AI builds, content generation, on-page SEO, citations

Friday: weekly report, junior VA review, planning the next week

Daily: monitor Goodcall calls across the portfolio, log leads, flag issues

Ongoing: link-building recommendations (you suggest, owner approves), kill-list hygiene, renter handoff support



YOU MUST HAVE



3+ years WordPress experience (GeneratePress, Elementor, or Gutenberg — at least one deeply)

2+ years on-page SEO (Rank Math or Yoast, schema, title/meta optimization, technical SEO basics)

1+ years using AI writing tools in production (Koala, Cuppa, Frase, SEOWriting, ChatGPT, or Claude)

Direct experience managing 30+ websites concurrently (in agency or in-house)

Comfort with citation tools (BrightLocal, Whitespark, or Loganix)

Fluent written English (we communicate primarily in Slack and Loom)

Ability to record clean, watchable Loom SOPs



NICE TO HAVE



Prior local SEO experience (Google Business Profile, NAP consistency, Maps Pack ranking)

Call tracking experience (CallRail, Goodcall, Twilio)

AI receptionist or chatbot setup experience (Goodcall, Synthflow, Stammer.ai)

Team-management experience (1-3 direct reports for 6+ months)

WP-CLI familiarity

Rank-tracking dashboards (SerpRobot, AccuRanker, Ahrefs Rank Tracker)



COMPENSATION & PERKS



$1,400-$1,800 USD per month based on experience (full-time, 40 hours/week)

13th-month bonus

All Philippines public holidays paid

5 days paid leave after 6 months tenure

Performance bonuses tied to portfolio KPIs (sites ranked, leads captured, renters signed)

Annual review with raise potential

Long-term role — we want someone who grows with us for 3+ years



TRIAL



1-week paid trial at full pay

Trial task: build one complete WordPress site end-to-end using our SOPs (provided on Day 1)

Pass criteria are clear and shared before the trial starts

Pass = full-time offer



WHO YOU'LL WORK WITH

You'll report directly to the founder. You'll have weekly 1:1 calls, daily async communication via Slack and Loom, and full ownership of your domain. We use Notion for SOPs, 1Password for credentials, and Google Workspace for
 and shared sheets.

WHO THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR



Anyone juggling 3+ other full-time clients (we need real focus)

Anyone uncomfortable with video communication (Loom is essential here)

Anyone who wants strict scripted tasks with no judgment calls

Anyone who hasn't actually managed multiple WordPress sites before



HOW TO APPLY

Reply to this post with all five of the following items. Applications missing any of these will not be reviewed.



A short paragraph (3-5 sentences) about your most recent SEO or WordPress role — what you owned, what you delivered, and what you would do differently if you started over

Your DISC scores in the format D/I/S/C (we use this to assess working-style fit, not to filter)

A 2-minute Loom video walking through ONE WordPress site you built or managed — point out the SEO work you did, the tools you used, and the results you achieved

Your monthly salary expectation in USD

The exact line "I read the entire job post" — so we know you actually read this



Looking forward to meeting our next teammate.

title: Amazon AI Systems Manager
description:
Role Overview

This role is responsible for designing AI-driven workflows, agents, and execution systems that generate:



Listings

Ad frameworks

Creative direction

Launch plans

External traffic flows



You will not be hired to write copy manually.

You will be hired to build systems that produce high-quality outputs repeatedly using AI, primarily through Claude (CoWork, Design, and Code).

This is a builder role, not a task-execution role.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Key Responsibilities

1. AI Workflow & Agent Development (Core Responsibility)



Design and build scalable systems using Claude:



Claude CoWork

Build structured workflows for:

Listing generation

A+ content frameworks

Launch planning

Standardize prompts and outputs across brands

Claude Design

Create systems that generate:

Ad concepts

Image frameworks

Creative briefs

Ensure outputs are consistent, usable, and production-ready

Claude Code

Develop lightweight logic and structured outputs to:

Automate workflows

Format large-scale outputs

Improve speed and consistency



???? Your job is to turn AI into an internal production engine.



2. Amazon Launch Systems Execution

Build repeatable launch frameworks, including:

Pre-launch readiness systems

Listing deployment workflows

Early ranking support structures

Support execution of launches using the systems you create

Sessions

CTR

Conversion rate



3. External Traffic System Integration

TikTok

Meta Ads Manager

Create:

Creator brief templates

Hook libraries

Traffic ? listing flow structures



???? Focus is on systemizing traffic, not manually managing campaigns.



4. Listing & Conversion System Design

Build systems that generate:

Titles

Bullets

A+ structures

FAQs

Ensure outputs align with:

Customer intent

Conversion psychology

Objection handling



???? You are building the machine — not typing the outputs.



5. Creative System Development

Build frameworks that generate:

Ad image structures (Ads 2–9 systems)

Copy + visual direction combinations

Use tools like Canva or Figma to prototype system outputs

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------



Required Qualifications

Amazon Experience (Non-negotiable)

2+ years experience with Amazon Seller Central

Hands-on exposure to:

Listings

A+ content

Product launches

Product Launch Experience

Direct involvement in product launches

Strong understanding of:

Indexing phase

Traffic-driven ranking

Conversion impact on growth

AI Systems Expertise (Critical Requirement)



Advanced use of Claude, including:



CoWork – building structured workflows

Design – generating creative systems

Code – structuring outputs and automating logic



Must be able to:



Build repeatable prompt systems

Create scalable workflows

Design AI agents or structured processes

Improve output quality over time



???? This is the most important requirement in the role.



External Traffic Understanding

Familiarity with:

TikTok

Meta Ads Manager

Understanding of:

How traffic impacts ranking

How to structure traffic flows

Creative & Systems Thinking

Ability to think in frameworks, not tasks

Comfortable with:



Canva

Figma (preferred)



Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

Number of AI workflows and systems successfully deployed

Reduction in manual work across brand operations

Speed of launch execution using systems

Consistency and quality of AI-generated outputs

Contribution to improved conversion rates

Scalability of systems across multiple brands



What Success Looks Like

AI systems are actively producing listings, ads, and launch frameworks

Launches are faster, more structured, and repeatable

Output quality improves over time through system refinement

Team reliance on manual work is significantly reduced

External traffic and listing systems are aligned and scalable

Work Style & Expectations

Builder mindset — creates systems, not tasks

High ownership with minimal supervision

Strong problem-solving and iteration ability

Moves fast and improves continuously

Obsessed with efficiency and scalability

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Summary



This role is for someone who understands Amazon, has real exposure to product launches, and can build AI-powered systems that scale execution.

You are not writing the work.

You are building the system that produces the work — repeatedly, efficiently, and at scale.



If you believe you have the skills and experience for this challenging role, reply with the headline "Amazon AI Wizard" to :







Thank you!

Tam Ma

Co Founder - Prodigy Media

mbers

We are a fast-growing agency with strong 
ntum.
