title: Remote Helpdesk Assistant
description:
We are looking for an entry-level Remote Helpdesk Assistant to join our growing team. This role primarily supports customer calls and basic helpdesk tasks and includes light social media posting as an added responsibility. 



Key Responsibilities



Helpdesk Support 



Answer incoming customer calls during assigned shift

Provide assistance, basic troubleshooting, and general support (training provided)

Help reduce reliance on our overflow call center

Respond to customer messages received through social media platforms



Basic Social Media Tasks 



Create simple posts using existing branding and guidelines

Publish posts to existing social media platforms

Respond to any inquiries generated from posts

No specific software required—any basic tools are acceptable

title: Tool Sourcing Specialist
description:
Looking for a talented Online Arbitrage (OA) Virtual Assistant (VA) for long-term work and growth. We are specifically looking for a Tool sourcing specialist/expert. We expect you to be pre trained and have prior experience sourcing Tools. 



We are a US-based e-commerce company doing multi-millions in sales per year on Amazon. We have been consistently scaling for 5 years. We have a strong and collaborative team that values consistency and results.   

 

POSITION DETAILS:          

- Pay: $1000/month (PHP 60,000) + opportunities for bonuses  

- WORK FROM HOME (flexible schedule)  

- Full-time: 40 hours/week

- Pay schedule: Bi-weekly



MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES:

- Source profitable online arbitrage products for Amazon (manual sourcing)

- Record leads in Google Sheets (clean and organized) 

- Evaluate deals quickly and accurately (profitability + competition) 

 

REQUIRED EXPERIENCE:

- Amazon Product Sourcing / Deal Sourcing

- SellerAmp / Keepa

- Fast and reliable internet connection

- Sourcing Tools 

 

HOW TO APPLY (5 MINUTES): 

Complete the short Google Form below (Bitly link goes to a Google Form). Only google form submissions will be considered.





We’re looking for a highly capable sourcing specialist and are prepared to pay competitively for strong performance. Please apply only if you have prior OA experience.



We are hiring ASAP and are looking to get you started within the next 2 weeks. Please do not reply to this post.

title: Administrative Manager
description:
Administrative Manager (Full-Time | Remote | Residential Construction)



Schedule: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM Pacific Time

Employment Type: Full-Time (40 hours/week)



About Us:

We are a growing residential construction and development company focused on high-quality infill housing projects. We are a small, high-performing team that takes pride in delivering exceptional results for our clients. Every tea
mber plays a meaningful role and is trusted to take ownership of their work. We value people who are highly organized, detail-oriented, proactive, and comfortable working independently within well-defined systems and processes. Our culture is built around accountability, clear communication, and supporting one another so everyone can perform at a high level. This role is best suited for someone who enjoys taking initiative, keeping things organized, and being part of a professional team that holds itself to a high standard.



Compensation:

We offer competitive full-time compensation based on experience and performance. We value long-term tea
mbers and reward reliability, strong performance, and growth within the company. This position is best suited for someone looking for stable, long-term work with a professional team, not short-term freelance work.



Position Overview:

We are hiring a full-time Administrative Manager to oversee and coordinate the administrative operations of our company. This role serves as the organizational backbone of the team—managing communication, scheduling, documentation, and internal coordination while ensuring a smooth and professional experience for both our team and our clients.



Key Responsibilities:

• Manage company and executive
 inboxes and calendars

• Communicate with U.S.-based customers by phone and

• Schedule consultations, meetings, and follow-ups

• Maintain organized digital files and documentation

• Track compliance items such as insurance certificates, lien releases, and tax filings

• Monitor deadlines and follow up with tea
mbers to keep projects moving

• Lead weekly tea
etings and help maintain accountability across the team

• Help improve and maintain company systems, organization, and workflows



Required Qualifications:

• Excellent spoken and written English

• Proficiency in Microsoft Word & Excel

• Highly organized and detail-oriented

• Able to work independently and manage responsibilities proactively



When applying, please address your application message to David, and begin your application message with the phrase Systems Create Success. Also please include a 30–60 second video recording of yourself describing your relevant work experience.



Thank you!

We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Operations & Scheduling Coordinator - Construction Experience
description:
Job Title: Operations & Scheduling Coordinator 

Position Type: Full-time

Working hours: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM Eastern Daylight Time, Monday - Friday

Rate: TBD





Key Responsibilities:

-Project Readiness Review: Evaluate each job using our “Vet for Production” checklist toconfirm all materials, approvals, and documentation are in place.

-Scheduling Coordination: Arrange project start dates with clients, field crews, and delivery vendors, ensuring smooth and timely execution.

-Logistics Management: Communicate site prep requirements, delivery windows, material drop details, and access instructions to all stakeholders.

-Status Tracking: Update job statuses and detailed notes in the CRM, ensuring the team is always up to date on project progress.

-Issue Flagging: Promptly alert the team of any job delays caused by missing materials, pending approvals, weather, or other external factors.





Requirements:

-Minimum 1 year of experience in project coordination, scheduling, or administrative roles, preferably in U.S.-based construction, insurance, or service industries.

-Proven experience managing multiple projects and coordinating schedules across diverse teams.

-Strong communication skills with the ability to liaise professionally with clients, vendors, and internal teams.

-Proficient in task management tools, spreadsheets, and Google Calendar.

-Adept at prioritizing tasks and working independently in a remote environment.

-Familiarity with construction or insurance-based project workflows.

-Experience using CRM systems and vetting documentation for operational readiness.





How to Apply?

-Send me a google drive link with a pdf/word copy of your resume. Make sure it's accessible

title: CPA /Senior Accountant
description:
Are you ready to take your accounting career to the next level? We are a US-based real estate investment and management company seeking a talented and motivated Senior Accountant to join our growing international team. This is a remote, full-time role based in the Philippines, where you’ll work directly with our US leadership during US business hours.

If you’re passionate about real estate accounting, thrive in fast-paced environments, and want to contribute to exciting projects across multiple entities, this is the opportunity for you.



What You’ll Do

As our Senior Accountant, you’ll be a key player in keeping our financial operations accurate, compliant, and efficient. You won’t just be crunching numbers—you’ll be driving clarity and insights that help us grow.

Manage the full accounting cycle, including journal entries, reconciliations, and month/quarter-end close.

Prepare accurate financial statements and management reports.

Handle accounts payable/receivable, tenant ledgers, and vendor payments

Oversee bank reconciliations and investigate discrepancies.

Assist with budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis.

Partner with CPAs during audits and tax filings.

Ensure compliance with US GAAP, Cash, and Modified Cash standards.

Maintain accurate financial data in AppFolio and QuickBooks Online (QBO).

Provide insightful reporting and analysis to the CFO and leadership team.



What We’re Looking For

We want someone who is not only technically strong but also proactive, communicative, and eager to add value.



Required:

Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field.

3–5 years of accounting experience with a US-based real estate company.

Strong knowledge of property management and hospitality accounting.

Hands-on experience with AppFolio and QuickBooks Online (QBO).

Excellent English communication skills, both written and spoken.

Meticulous attention to detail, organizational strength, and reliability in meeting deadlines.

Flexibility to work US business hours.



Preferred:

CPA license (Philippines or US).

Experience with multi-entity structures and consolidations.

Familiarity with investor reporting and distributions.

Strong Excel skills, especially for financial modeling and analysis.



Why You’ll Love Working With Us

Competitive pay with long-term career growth opportunities.

A chance to work directly with US executives and decision-makers.

Exposure to exciting real estate and hospitality projects across the US.

A professional yet supportive team culture where your work is valued.

A stable role in an expanding international company with room to grow.



Here, your skills will make an immediate impact, and your career will grow alongside our company’s success.



If you’re ready to take ownership, make a difference, and build a rewarding career in real estate accounting, we’d love to meet you.



Apply now and become an essential part of our accounting team.

title: People & Client Success Lead
description:
Job Timing: 9am -6pm MST US Time Zone

Location: Remote

Employment Type: Full-Time



About the Company

Wadic is committed to delivering exceptional services and building meaningful relationships with both our employees and clients. We believe that our people are our greatest asset, and client satisfaction is the cornerstone of our success.

Job Summary

We are looking for a dynamic and proactive People & Client Success Lead to oversee employee engagement, HR operations, and client relationship management. This role bridges internal people management and external client success, ensuring a productive work environment and outstanding customer experiences.

The ideal candidate will have strong leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills, with experience in HR practices, account management, and client retention strategies.



Key Responsibilities

People Operations & Employee Success:



Lead recruitment, onboarding, and offboarding processes.

Develop and implement employee engagement initiatives to improve morale and retention.

Manage employee relations, conflict resolution, and performance improvement plans.

Maintain HR policies, employee records, and compliance with labor laws.

Coordinate training and development programs for team growth.

Monitor attendance, leaves, and overall workforce productivity.

Foster a positive workplace culture aligned with company values.



Client Success & Relationship Management:



Serve as the primary point of contact for key clients.

Build strong relationships with clients to understand their goals and expectations.

Ensure successful onboarding and implementation of services for new clients.

Proactively address client concerns and provide timely resolutions.

Track client satisfaction, retention, and renewal metrics.

Conduct regular check-ins, QBRs (Quarterly Business Reviews), and feedback sessions.

Collaborate with internal teams to ensure timely delivery of services and solutions.



Leadership & Strategy:



Develop strategies to improve employee satisfaction and client retention.

Analyze KPIs related to team performance and client success.

Prepare reports on employee engagement, turnover, and client satisfaction.

Work cross-functionally with sales, operations, and management teams.

Identify process improvement opportunities to enhance efficiency.



Requirements:



Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Management, or a related field.

3 or more years of experience in HR, people operations, client success, or account management.

Strong knowledge of HR practices and employee engagement strategies.

Experience managing client accounts and maintaining long-term relationships.

Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.

Ability to handle confidential information professionally.

Proficiency in CRM, HRMS, and project management tools.

Leadership and decision-making skills.



Preferred Qualifications:



Experience in a service-based or tech company.

Familiarity with Agile or fast-paced work environments.

HR certifications or client success/account management certifications are a plus.



Benefits:



Competitive salary package

Professional development opportunities

title: Sales Representative (Remote – Philippines)
description:
Company: Buddy Moving

Location: Remote (Philippines-based)

Hours: Full-time, aligned with U.S. business hours

Compensation: Hourly + performance-based incentives

Type: Long-term position



About the Role



Buddy Moving is seeking a highly motivated Sales Representative to serve as the primary point of contact for prospective customers. This is a sales-driven role, not a customer service position. You will be responsible for converting inbound leads into booked moves by building trust, communicating clearly, and confidently guiding customers through the booking process.



We are looking for someone who is upbeat, articulate, and persuasive, with a strong ability to connect with U.S.-based customers and close sales consistently.



Key Responsibilities

Answer inbound calls, texts, and
s from prospective customers

Qualify leads and identify customer needs quickly and effectively

Provide accurate quotes and explain services with confidence

Follow up with unbooked leads to convert them into booked jobs

Handle objections professionally and guide customers toward a decision

Maintain accurate records and notes in the CRM system

Meet or exceed booking and revenue targets



Required Qualifications

Excellent spoken and written English with clear pronunciation

Strong sales skills with a proven ability to close deals

Confident, upbeat, and professional phone presence

Experience speaking with U.S.-based customers preferred

Ability to build rapport quickly and communicate persuasively

Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace



Preferred Experience

Sales or call center experience (inbound or outbound)

Experience in home services, logistics, or moving companies

Familiarity with CRM systems and scheduling tools

Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets



Compensation & Incentives:



Base Pay: $6.00 – $9.00 per hour (depending on experience and performance)

Commission: Additional Performance-based commission on booked and completed jobs

Earnings Potential: Strong performers typically earn additional monthly commission based on sales volume



How to Apply



To be considered, please submit the following:



A brief introduction about yourself

Your relevant sales experience and any performance metrics

Your availability and time zone

A 3–5 minute Loom video introducing yourself and explaining why you are a strong fit for this sales role



Note: Applicants must be confident speaking with U.S.-based customers and comfortable working in a sales-focused environment.

title: Virtual Assistant for Temporary Accommodation Business
description:
Are you highly organised, detail-oriented, and a natural problem-solver? Would you like to be part of a fast-growing property business that provides vital housing for those who need it most?



We’re looking for a reliable Virtual Assistant to join our team and support the daily operations of our Temporary Accommodation (TA) business in the UK. 



This is a remote role and perfect for someone with strong communication skills and solid admin experience — ideally within property, lettings, or housing.





 About Us:



We provide safe, fully furnished, and well-managed accommodation to councils, housing associations, and vulnerable individuals across the UK. With multiple properties under management and a growing network of housing partnerships, we aim to scale fast — and we need a strong right hand to help run the backend operations.







Role: Virtual Assistant



Location: Remote (UK-based preferred)

Hours: Part-time (25–30 hours per week to start)

Rate: Competitive hourly rate based on experience

Start Date: Immediate





Responsibilities:



Tenant Management & Support

 • Respond to tenant messages, maintenance issues, and complaints

 • Coordinate emergency repairs and follow up with contractors

 • Check tenant wellbeing by
 or WhatsApp as needed

 • Update tenant records and ensure compliance with contracts



Bookings & Move-ins

 • Schedule and manage tenant check-ins and check-outs

 • Create welcome packs and send move-in instructions

 • Chase missing documents (IDs, proof of benefit, etc.)

 • Liaise with local housing officers to coordinate placements



Admin & Compliance

 • Upload property certificates and documents to internal systems

 • Ensure EPCs, gas safety, EICRs, and HMO licenses are up to date

 • Track key dates (rent due dates, inspections, renewals)

 • Maintain Excel trackers and Google Sheets



Landlord & Council Liaison

 • Send rent statements to landlords

 • Update councils on room availability

 • Assist in preparing invoices and chasing overdue payments



General Operations

 • Set up systems and automations where needed

 • Support with CRM updates and task lists

 • Order furniture, bedding, and essentials when needed

 • Carry out basic internet research for new partnerships or suppliers







What We’re Looking For:

 • Proven experience in property, lettings, social housing, or admin

 • Strong written and spoken English

 • Excellent organisation and time management skills

 • Ability to stay calm under pressure and juggle multiple tasks

 • Familiarity with Google Workspace, Zoom, WhatsApp, and task managers (e.g. Trello or Asana)

 • Proactive and solution-focused attitude — you don’t wait to be told what to do





Bonus If You Have:

 • Experience working with local authorities or social housing tenants

 • Understanding of compliance requirements in property management

 • Knowledge of CRM systems like Podio, 
, or Notion







 Why Join Us?

 • Flexible remote working

 • Purpose-driven work — we help vulnerable people find homes

 • Room to grow with a scaling business

 • Direct mentorship and training provided







How to Apply:

Send your CV and a short cover letter explaining why you’re the perfect fit for this role to [your
 here].

Please include “VA Application – Temporary Accommodation” in the subject line





We look forward to hear from you!

title: Remote Inventory Analyst
description:
Company: Gizmos Group of Companies

Department: Inventory & Supply Chain

Reports To: Inventory & Supply Chain Manager

Location: Remote (Full-Time)



COMPANY CONTEXT

The Gizmos Group is a leading retail and distribution company in Guyana, operating across

multiple divisions including electronics, beauty, duty-free, and distribution. With multiple retail

outlets and a centralized warehouse (“Bond”), the company is focused on building a world-class,

data-driven inventory system that supports growth, efficiency, and profitability.

The Remote Inventory Analyst plays a mission-critical role in achieving this vision



ROLE PURPOSE

The Remote Inventory Analyst is responsible for owning and controlling inventory intelligence

across all locations.

This role ensures:

• The right products are in the right stores at the right time

• Stock is proactively replenished, not reactively requested

• Inventory is balanced, optimized, and fully visible

• All stock movements are accurately recorded and controlled in Lightspeed Retail POS

This position replaces guesswork with data-driven decision-making



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

1. Inventory Monitoring & Analysis (DAILY CONTROL)

• Monitor stock levels across:

• Bond (central warehouse)

• All retail outlets

• Analyze:

• Sales velocity per SKU per location

• Days of stock cover

• Stock-out risks

• Overstock / slow-moving items

• Identify:

• Gaps in stock availability

• Imbalances between locations

• Dead stock risks



2. Automated Replenishment Management

• Proactively initiate:

• Bond ? Store transfers

• Store ? Store transfers

• Ensure:

• Stores are replenished based on data, not manual requests

• Minimum and maximum stock levels are maintained

• Maintain and continuously improve:

• Replenishment logic (min/max levels)

• Stock coverage targets (e.g., 2–4 weeks)



3. Purchase Order (PO) Creation & Supplier Coordination

• Create all supplier POs in the system based on:

• Supplier invoices / proformas

• Reorder analysis

• Ensure:

• POs are created in advance of shipment arrival

• All incoming goods are properly recorded

• Track:

• PO status (ordered, in transit, received)

• Supplier timelines

• Missing or short-shipped items

• Coordinate with:

• Finance (for payment processing)

• Warehouse (for receiving readiness)



4. Pre-Allocation of Incoming Inventory

• Analyze incoming shipments BEFORE arrival

• Allocate quantities across:

• Retail outlets

• Bond stock

• Create transfer plans so that:

• Stores can see stock “in transit”

• Warehouse can execute dispatch immediately upon arrival



5. Store Stock Optimization

• Continuously monitor:

• Store-level stock performance

• Identify:

• Fast-moving SKUs needing replenishment

• Slow-moving SKUs requiring redistribution

• Execute:

• Inter-store transfers to optimize sell-through



6. Stock Health & Performance Reporting

Generate and maintain daily dashboards including:

• Out-of-stock report

• Low stock alerts

• Overstock / dead stock report

• Inventory valuation

• Sell-through rates

• Stock aging analysis

• Open POs and pending transfers



7. Inventory Accuracy & Control

• Monitor and enforce:

• Accurate stock levels across all locations

• Review:

• Daily store stock counts

• Warehouse cycle counts

• Investigate:

• Variances and discrepancies

• Negative stock situations

• Flag issues immediately to management



8. Transfer Management & Tracking

• Create and track all stock transfers:

• Ensure proper documentation and system entry

• Monitor:

• Transfers not yet received

• Delays in dispatch or confirmation

• Ensure:

• Full visibility of stock in transit



9. Loss Prevention & Cost Control

• Identify:

• Shrinkage patterns

• Unusual stock movements

• Repeated discrepancies by location

• Support:

• Return-to-supplier processes

• Supplier claims for missing goods

• Ensure:

• No unauthorized stock adjustments



10. System Integrity & Process Discipline

• Maintain strict discipline in:

• PO creation

• Stock transfers

• Inventory records

• Enforce:

• No manual stock entries outside system processes

• Continuously improve:

• Inventory workflows

• Reporting accuracy

• Data cleanliness



SYSTEMS & TOOLS

• Lightspeed Retail POS (Primary system)

• Microsoft Excel / Google Sheets (Advanced level required)

• Inventory dashboards and reporting tools

• Communication tools 
, WhatsApp, Slack)



DAILY / WEEKLY WORKFLOW EXPECTATIONS

Daily:

• Review stock levels across all outlets

• Trigger replenishment transfers

• Monitor open POs and transfers

• Review stock discrepancies

• Update dashboards and alerts



Weekly:

• Adjust min/max stock levels based on trends

• Conduct stock balancing across outlets

• Analyze dead stock and slow movers

• Submit performance reports to management



Monthly:

• Review overall inventory performance

• Recommend improvements in stock strategy

• Support financial stock valuation and reporting



KEY PERFORMANCE INDICATORS (KPIs)

• Stock availability rate (% of SKUs in stock)

• Reduction in stock-outs

• Inventory turnover rate

• Reduction in dead stock

• Accuracy of inventory (variance %)

• Timeliness of PO creation and transfers

• Reduction in negative stock occurrences

• Store replenishment efficiency



IDEAL CANDIDATE PROFILE

Education:

• Degree in Supply Chain, Business, Finance, or related field



Experience:

• 3–5+ years in inventory, supply chain, or retail analytics

• Experience managing multi-location inventory (preferred)

• Experience with POS/inventory systems (Lightspeed preferred)



Technical Skills:

• Advanced Excel (MANDATORY)

• Pivot tables

• Lookup functions

• Data analysis

• Strong analytical and numerical skills

• System-oriented thinker



Core Competencies:

• Highly analytical – able to interpret data and make decisions

• Detail-oriented – zero tolerance for errors

• Proactive – anticipates problems before they happen

• Organized – manages multiple SKUs, brands, and locations

• Accountable – takes ownership of inventory performance

• Process-driven – enforces discipline across the system



CRITICAL SUCCESS FACTORS

• Ability to work independently and remotely

• Strong communication with on-site teams

• Discipline in following structured processes

• Confidence to challenge inefficiencies and propose improvements

• High level of trust and integrity



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE IN THIS ROLE

• Stores are never out of key products

• Inventory is balanced across all locations

• Warehouse operates efficiently with clear transfer plans

• Management has full visibility of inventory at all times

• The company achieves higher sales with lower stock holding



STRATEGIC IMPORTANCE

This is not a support role it is a control tower position that directly impacts:

• Revenue (through product availability)

• Cash flow (through stock optimization)

• Profitability (through reduced waste and shrinkage)



Please submit your resume by applying directly through the job platform. We look forward to reviewing your application.

title: IT Product Manager
description:
Remote | Full-Time or Interim Contract | TO BE CONSIDERED, Apply at stafflyhq.ai/apply



We are hiring a Product Manager / Product Owner for a mature B2B SaaS company in the event management space. This company serves thousands of organizations and tens of millions of end users annually, and is currently rebuilding discipline around product execution, roadmap ownership, customer discovery, PRD quality, and engineering handoff.



This role is for a hands-on product operator, not a passive backlog manager. The right candidate can step into a complex software product, understand customer workflows quickly, write clear requirements, manage priorities, reduce ambiguity for engineering, and create enough product structure that the team can ship faster with fewer delays.



The company has been without a dedicated Product Manager for about a month. A designer is temporarily covering product responsibilities, but this is not a long-term solution. Product delivery has slowed because work is not being fully researched, scoped, documented, and defined before engineering begins.



The Mission



Your mission is to restore clarity, structure, and velocity to the product development process.



You will own the connection between customers, leadership, design, engineering, and the roadmap. You will be responsible for making sure the team knows what to build, why it matters, what is in scope, what is out of scope, how success will be measured, and what requirements must be satisfied before work is considered ready.



This role requires someone who can operate as both Product Manager and Product Owner in a lean SaaS environment.



What You Will Own



You will own the product roadmap at both the strategic and tactical levels. This includes understanding company priorities, current roadmap items, delayed initiatives, customer needs, business goals, engineering constraints, and d
 what should be built now, later, or not at all.



You will own discovery. This means speaking directly with customers and users, reviewing customer success feedback, understanding event organizer workflows, identifying pain points, synthesizing voice of customer, and turning that information into product direction.



You will own PRDs. The client specifically needs someone who can consistently produce strong product requirements documents. Your PRDs must be detailed enough for design and engineering to move with confidence. They should include context, business goals, user problems, requirements, use cases, acceptance criteria, edge cases, dependencies, risks, scope, out-of-scope items, and testing considerations.



You will own product process. The company needs someone who can create and document repeatable processes around discovery, prioritization, requirements writing, sprint readiness, design handoff, engineering handoff, QA expectations, and release review.



You will own cross-functional alignment. You will work with leadership, roughly 10 developers, designers, customer-facing teams, and customers. You will participate in product planning, sprint planning, roadmap reviews, and regular product check-ins.



Core Responsibilities



Manage the product roadmap and translate company strategy into clear, prioritized product work.



Create detailed PRDs, user stories, acceptance criteria, requirements, process documentation, and implementation-ready product briefs.



Conduct customer interviews and internal stakeholder interviews to deeply understand user needs, product gaps, and business opportunities.



Synthesize customer feedback and communicate the “why” behind product decisions to leadership, design, engineering, and customer-facing teams.



Work with designers on wireframes, user flows, prototypes, and product experience decisions.



Work with engineers to clarify tickets, resolve ambiguity, define acceptance criteria, manage scope, support sprint planning, and unblock implementation questions.



Act as Product Owner by ensuring tickets are clear, prioritized, scoped, and ready before engineering begins.



Improve the quality of upfront product definition so development moves faster and scope changes are reduced.



Help establish stronger QA expectations, especially in an environment where formal QA is limited and testing is currently handled mostly by internal tea
mbers.



Use tools such as Linear for ticket management and Notion for documentation. Experience with Linear is helpful, but experience with Jira, ClickUp, Shortcut, Asana, or similar tools is acceptable.



Support weekly product planning, two-week sprint cycles, monthly reviews, quarterly roadmap planning, and ongoing product scorecard discussions.



Required Experience



3+ years of product management experience in SaaS, software, or platform-based products.



Strong experience writing detailed PRDs, user stories, acceptance criteria, and product documentation.



Proven experience working directly with engineering teams in sprint-based development environments.



Strong customer discovery experience, including direct customer conversations.



Ability to synthesize customer needs, business priorities, and technical constraints into clear product decisions.



Experience managing a roadmap, backlog, and product prioritization process.



Ability to create structure where product process is incomplete, inconsistent, or underdeveloped.



Strong written communication skills. This role is documentation-heavy.



Strong verbal communication skills. You must be able to explain decisions clearly across leadership, design, engineering, and customer-facing teams.



Ability to work independently with minimal hand-holding.



Comfort working remotely and asynchronously with a distributed team.



Ability to ramp quickly into a complex, mature product with a large surface area.



Preferred Experience



Experience with B2B SaaS products.



Experience with event management software, scheduling software, ticketing platforms, workflow platforms, CRM systems, community platforms, marketplace products, or tools with complex user workflows.



Experience with product-led growth, free trials, freemium products, or inbound SaaS growth motions.



Experience working on legacy products, modernization efforts, or products in technical transition.



Experience improving weak or informal product, QA, or delivery processes.



Experience partnering with design from rough wireframes through prototypes and final handoff.



Experience with AI-assisted product management workflows, including using AI to accelerate research, documentation, PRDs, analysis, and ticket writing.



Technical orientation is helpful, but this is not a deeply technical PM role. You do not need to be a technical product manager, but you must understand software development well enough to make engineers more effective.



This Role Is Not For You If



You only manage tickets and do not do customer discovery.



You avoid talking to customers.



You cannot write detailed PRDs.



You need every process handed to you before you can operate.



You are only a project manager and do not have product judgment.



You are only a designer or UX profile without true PM ownership.



You are uncomfortable working directly with engineers.



You cannot manage scope or push back on unclear requirements.



You require months before creating value.



You do not document decisions clearly.



You cannot explain what should be built, why it matters, and what should be deprioritized.



Success in the First 30 Days



You will learn the product, customer segments, current roadmap, major workflows, and existing backlog.



You will review existing Notion documentation and Linear tickets.



You will meet with leadership, design, engineering, and customer-facing stakeholders.



You will identify the biggest gaps in the current product process.



You will begin improving PRDs, tickets, and scope clarity for active work.



You will start reducing ambiguity before work reaches engineering.



Success in the First 60 Days



You will own active roadmap items.



You will lead discovery for priority features.



You will write detailed PRDs with acceptance criteria and clear scope.



You will improve ticket quality in Linear.



You will help reduce scope creep during development.



You will bring customer feedback into planning conversations.



You will support design handoff, prototype review, engineering clarification, and release readiness.



Success in the First 90 Days



You will be running the product process with minimal oversight.



You will own roadmap execution cadence.



You will support quarterly roadmap planning.



You will improve product delivery speed by tightening discovery, documentation, and handoff quality.



You will help the team ship with more confidence, less rework, and fewer avoidable delays.



Ideal Candidate



The ideal candidate is a hands-on SaaS Product Manager who can operate in a lean, fast-moving environment. You are strong in discovery, requirements writing, prioritization, roadmap execution, stakeholder communication, and engineering collaboration.



You know how to turn messy inputs into clear product direction. You can talk to customers, extract insights, define scope, write requirements, align design and engineering, and keep the team focused on what matters.



You are not waiting for someone else to create the product process. You are the person who creates it, documents it, improves it, and runs it.



Apply



To qualify for this role, you must apply at:



stafflyhq.ai/apply

title: Senior Automation Developer (n8n, Python & Claude Code)
description:
We are looking for a highly skilled Developer and Automation Expert to join our team and lead the design, development, and implementation of advanced, AI-driven automation systems for our company.



This role is not just about connecting tools; it requires a true developer at heart. If you write clean code, have deep hands-on experience with n8n and Python, and are already leveraging modern AI coding tools like Claude Code to build faster and smarter, we want you on our team.



Responsibilities



Design & Build: Architect, build, and maintain complex, highly reliable automation workflows using n8n.



Custom Development: Write and maintain custom Python scripts to handle complex data manipulation, custom API integrations, and backend processes that go beyond standard n8n capabilities.



AI Integration: Leverage Claude Code to develop, debug, and optimize code, automation processes, and custom scripts.



System Integration: Connect third-party tools, SaaS platforms, databases, and CRMs via REST APIs, Webhooks, and OAuth.



Optimization & Maintenance: Implement robust error handling, logging, retries, and performance optimization for all code and workflows.



Business Alignment: Translate business requirements and operational bottlenecks into technical, scalable automation logic.



Requirements (Must-Haves)



Software Development Background: Proven experience as a developer with a strong understanding of software engineering principles and system architecture.



Python Mastery: Strong, hands-on experience writing clean, efficient, and production-ready Python code. (This is a strict requirement).



n8n Mastery: Extensive experience building, deploying, and maintaining n8n workflows in a production environment. (This is a strict requirement).



Claude Code Expertise: Proven, hands-on experience using Claude Code for development, scripting, and building AI-assisted automations. (This is a strict requirement).



Technical Foundation: Deep understanding of APIs (REST), Webhooks, JSON, data structures, and conditional routing.



Mindset: High ownership, responsibility, and strong analytical problem-solving skills.



Nice to Have



Proficiency in JavaScript /



[Node.js](
)



(highly preferred for custom n8n nodes).



Experience with other automation platforms like Make (Integromat) or Zapier.



Experience integrating other AI APIs (OpenAI, Gemini, etc.).



Experience working with WhatsApp APIs, AI agents, or advanced CRM integrations.



Background working in a SaaS company or an automation agency.

title: AI-First Creative Strategist & Video Editor
description:
About NOURI AI

We're building an AI-powered app that scans supplements and tells you if they're legit or trash. Think of it as a Shazam for supplements. We're pre-launch, moving fast, and need someone who can create scroll-stopping content that converts.



The Role

We're looking for an AI-native Creative Strategist / Video Editor. The key differentiator: you should be using AI for 70%+ of the production pipeline — generating footage, creating avatars, automating edits. We want someone who ships fast because they've mastered the AI toolkit, not someone who manually edits frame by frame.



What You'll Do



• Create 10 short-form video ads per week for TikTok & Meta (Reels, Stories, Feed)

• Use AI as your primary production tool:

  • Video generation: Sora, Kling, Nano Banana for b-roll and scenes

  • AI avatars/presenters: Higgsfield for talking head content

  • Voice cloning/TTS: ElevenLabs for voiceovers

  • AI editing: CapCut auto-captions, AI templates, auto-cuts

• Develop creative concepts and hooks based on trending formats

• Scrape and source b-roll from the internet when AI generation isn't ideal

• Create multiple variations of each ad (different hooks, CTAs, lengths)

• Track what performs and double down on winners

• Stay on top of new AI tools — you should be testing new tools weekly



Requirements



• Experience creating short-form video content for paid social (Meta & TikTok)

• Heavy AI tool user — show us your AI workflow, not just your timeline

• Portfolio showing direct response / performance marketing video ads

• Strong understanding of hooks, retention, and what makes people stop scrolling

• Proficiency in CapCut (required)

• Already using at least 3+ AI video/audio tools regularly

• Can work in US Eastern timezone overlap (at least 4-5 hours)

• Self-starter — takes a brief and delivers without hand-holding

• Fluent English (written — for captions, hooks, CTAs)



Nice to Have



• Experience in health/wellness/supplement niche

• Understanding of Meta/TikTok Ads Manager (reading performance metrics)

• Experience building creative testing frameworks (hook matrices, angle testing)



Deliverables



• 10 edited video ads per week (mixed formats: 15s, 30s, 60s)

• 3-5 new hook/angle concepts per week

• Weekly creative report (what worked, what to test next)

• Organized asset library



How to Apply

Send us:



1. 3-5 examples of ads you made (bonus if AI-generated content is visible)

2. List of AI tools you use daily and how you use them

3. A 60-second Loom showing your AI-powered editing workflow

4. Your availability and monthly rate

description:

Process Mapping & Funnel Mapping

 that clearly outline workflows, ownership, dependencies, and handoffs *across teams

*Develop funnel maps that document the full customer journey—from traffic source to conversion, follow-up, and retention

title: Marketing & Podcast Coordinator
description:

, and web platforms. The ideal candidate is a strong communicator with excellent project management skills and a passion for content creation, audience engagement, and digital storytelling.

Podcast Production

YouTube full-length episodes

, LinkedIn, Instagram)

, and website platforms

Support

Familiarity with

title: Video Content Creation + Admin VA
description:
We are a growing YouTube & Tiktok-based content business looking for one specific type of person: 



> Someone who is highly detail-oriented, follows systems precisely, and can edit talking head and faceless videos to a professional standard.



This is NOT a general VA role. Video editing is the core of this position — 90% of what you will do. If you cannot edit cleanly, follow a workflow exactly as shown, and QA (Quality Review) your own work before submission, this role is not for you.



?  Read carefully. This role requires a specific skill set and mindset. If you're the right fit, you'll know it immediately.

__________________________________



THE ROLE

You will edit talking head and faceless educational videos using CapCut desktop (paid/Pro version required). You will follow a specific workflow that has been developed and refined for this business. You will QA your own work during editing — not after — and submit clean videos that do not require correction.



You will also handle supporting admin tasks once video editing is mastered.

___________________________________



VIDEO EDITING - PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITY (85%)

This is the most important part of the role. Everything else is secondary.

•  Edit talking head and faceless videos in CapCut desktop (Pro version required)

•  Follow a precise 3-step workflow: cut audio ? sync video ? QA simultaneously during editing

•  Extract audio from video clips and manage audio/video tracks separately on the timeline

•  Remove mistakes, pauses, breaths, and dead air with clean, undetectable cuts

•  Ensure no words are cut off — every word must complete fully before a cut

•  Eliminate all screen jumps — mouse movement must be natural and continuous across every clip transition

•  Ensure all audio clips are directly adjacent on the timeline — no gaps

•  QA during editing, not after — you should know your video is clean before you submit it

•  Complete a full QA checklist before every submission

•  Target turnaround: 20-minute video edited and submitted within 4 hours

•  Add end screens, CTAs, captions, thumbnails, and descriptions for YouTube and TikTok

* Add graphics, text, pop-ups and motion graphics on video

___________________________________



ADMIN/OPERATIONS SUPPORT - SECONDARY RESPONSIBILITY (15%)



•  Social media research and engagement using provided SOPs

•  Community management (comments, conversation starters)

•  Manage tasks and deadlines in Asana and Slack

•  Update sales systems, lead information, and website content

•  Proofread and update documents and spreadsheets accurately

•  Daily and weekly reporting - research and data entry



________________________________



MUST HAVE

If you cannot meet all of the following, do not apply.

•  CapCut desktop — paid/Pro version — installed and ready before start date

•  Able to edit a 20-minute talking head video within 4 hours once trained

•  Available to work EST hours (evenings 6–9 PM EST for training for the first 2-3 weeks, daytime EST after training)

•  Follows workflows exactly as taught — no substituting your own methods

•  Takes detailed notes during training and does not ask repeated questions

•  Submits a completed QA checklist with every video — no exceptions

•  Does not submit work with obvious errors — you catch your own mistakes

________________________________



YOU'RE A GREAT FIT IF YOU HAVE

•  A background that required extreme attention to detail — QA/compliance, medical billing, insurance operations, paralegal, data auditing, finance, claims processing, or similar

•  Proven experience editing talking head or faceless YouTube videos

•  A structured, analytical mindset — you think critically when something looks or sounds wrong

•  Strong time management — you complete work on deadline without dragging hours

•  CapCut desktop experience (required) — Adobe Premiere or Final Cut Pro is a plus

•  Experience with Asana, Notion, ClickUp, or Slack

•  Canva experience for thumbnails and basic graphics



COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

$3–5 USD/hour depending on experience and performance after training ($2.50 Per hour during training)

•  Paid weekly

•  13th-month pay

•  Monthly performance bonuses for exceeding KPIs

•  Annual retention bonus review

•  Performance-based raises

•  Promotion path — top performers can grow into team lead roles

•  Ongoing skill training (AI, social media, video editing, copywriting, and more)

•  Stable, long-term employment with clear systems, SOPs, and feedback

___________________________



HOW TO APPLY - READ EVERY STEP

Send an
 to 
 with the subject line:

"Applying To – Video Editor VA – [Your Name]"



Include ALL of the following in your
:



1. Your OnlineJobs.ph profile link

2. Resume (PDF)

3. A 1–2 minute intro video: tell us who you are, your editing experience, and what makes you the right fit for this specific role

4. A video editing sample — ideally a talking head or faceless video you have edited. No sample = no consideration.

5. Confirm you have CapCut desktop Pro installed and ready

6. Confirm you are available EST hours

7. Put the word EAGLE at the end of your
 so we know you read this in full



*Applications that do not follow these steps exactly will not be reviewed. Attention to detail starts with how you apply.

title: Analytical VA — Video Editing + Admin Support
description:
Detail-Driven Analytical VA

Video Production + Admin + Graphics

Remote  |  Philippines-based  |  Full-Time  |  EST Hours



Before you read the task list, read this first.



We are not looking for someone with the longest resume or the most software certifications. We are looking for a specific type of person.



You catch things other people miss.

You follow a process exactly as given — you don't improvise or substitute your own method.

When you finish a task, you review it before anyone asks you to.

You take detailed notes the first time something is taught so you don't need it repeated.

When you hit a problem, you think through it before asking for help.

You take ownership of your output — errors in your work are your responsibility to catch, not your employer's.



If that description sounds like you, keep reading. If it doesn't, this role will be frustrating for both of us.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are a growing YouTube-based content business producing talking head and faceless educational videos. This is an operations support role — you will help keep the content engine running cleanly and efficiently.



The role has three areas. They are listed in order of priority:



•  Video editing — this is the core of the role and where your attention to detail matters most

•  Admin and operations support

•  Graphics and visual content



You do not need to be an expert in all three from day one. You need to have the right mindset and be trainable. The workflow for video editing will be taught to you in full. What cannot be taught is the ability to follow it precisely and catch your own mistakes.



VIDEO EDITING — PRIMARY FOCUS

?  This is the most important part of the role. Everything else is secondary to this.

You will edit talking head and faceless YouTube videos using CapCut desktop (Pro version required). You will be trained on a specific 3-step workflow. Your job is to learn it, apply it exactly, and deliver clean videos that do not need correction.



What that looks like in practice:

•  Cut audio, sync video, and QA simultaneously — not separately, not after

•  No words cut off mid-syllable — every word completes fully before a cut

•  No screen jumps — mouse movement must be natural and continuous across every clip

•  No audio gaps — all clips butted directly together on the timeline

•  Audio extracted and managed separately from the video track

•  Complete a QA checklist before every submission — no exceptions

•  Target turnaround: 20-minute video edited and submitted within 4 hours once trained



You do not need years of editing experience. You need CapCut desktop familiarity, a trained eye for detail, and the ability to absorb and apply a workflow the first time it is shown to you.



ADMIN & OPERATIONS SUPPORT

•  Social media research and engagement using provided SOPs

•  Community management — comments, conversation starters, engagement

•  Manage and track tasks in Asana and Slack

•  Update sales systems, lead tracking, and website content as needed

•  Data entry, spreadsheet tracking, and document management

•  Daily and weekly reporting



GRAPHICS & VISUAL CONTENT

•  Create thumbnails and social media graphics using Canva

•  Maintain consistency with brand templates and visual standards

•  Design basic promotional and content visuals as directed



WHO YOU ARE

The best candidates for this role have come from backgrounds that demanded precision — not just creativity. If your prior work required you to get things exactly right with no room for error, you'll fit here.



Backgrounds that translate well:

•  QA or compliance

•  Medical billing or insurance operations

•  Data entry auditing or claims processing

•  Paralegal or legal support

•  Finance, payroll, or bookkeeping

•  Social media management with a strong analytical component

•  Any role where following a system exactly was non-negotiable



You also need:

•  CapCut desktop — Pro/paid version — installed before your start date

•  Availability EST hours — training is 6–9 PM EST, flexible after probation

•  Strong English communication — written and verbal

•  Experience with Asana, Notion, ClickUp, or similar tools

COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

•  Paid weekly

•  13th-month pay

•  Monthly performance bonuses for exceeding KPIs

•  Annual retention bonus review

•  Performance-based raises

•  Promotion path — top performers can grow into team lead and management roles

title: Detail-Driven Video Operations VA
description:
Detail-Driven Operations VA

Video Production + Admin + Graphics

Remote  |  Philippines-based  |  Full-Time  |  EST Hours



Before you read the task list, read this first.



We are not looking for someone with the longest resume or the most software certifications. We are looking for a specific type of person.



You catch things other people miss.

You follow a process exactly as given — you don't improvise or substitute your own method.

When you finish a task, you review it before anyone asks you to.

You take detailed notes the first time something is taught so you don't need it repeated.

When you hit a problem, you think through it before asking for help.

You take ownership of your output — errors in your work are your responsibility to catch, not your employer's.



If that description sounds like you, keep reading. If it doesn't, this role will be frustrating for both of us.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are a growing YouTube-based content business producing talking head and faceless educational videos. This is an operations support role — you will help keep the content engine running cleanly and efficiently.



The role has three areas. They are listed in order of priority:



•  Video editing — this is the core of the role and where your attention to detail matters most

•  Admin and operations support

•  Graphics and visual content



You do not need to be an expert in all three from day one. You need to have the right mindset and be trainable. The workflow for video editing will be taught to you in full. What cannot be taught is the ability to follow it precisely and catch your own mistakes.



VIDEO EDITING — PRIMARY FOCUS

?  This is the most important part of the role. Everything else is secondary to this.

You will edit talking head and faceless YouTube videos using CapCut desktop (Pro version required). You will be trained on a specific 3-step workflow. Your job is to learn it, apply it exactly, and deliver clean videos that do not need correction.



What that looks like in practice:

•  Cut audio, sync video, and QA simultaneously — not separately, not after

•  No words cut off mid-syllable — every word completes fully before a cut

•  No screen jumps — mouse movement must be natural and continuous across every clip

•  No audio gaps — all clips butted directly together on the timeline

•  Audio extracted and managed separately from the video track

•  Complete a QA checklist before every submission — no exceptions

•  Target turnaround: 20-minute video edited and submitted within 4 hours once trained



You do not need years of editing experience. You need CapCut desktop familiarity, a trained eye for detail, and the ability to absorb and apply a workflow the first time it is shown to you.



ADMIN & OPERATIONS SUPPORT

•  Social media research and engagement using provided SOPs

•  Community management — comments, conversation starters, engagement

•  Manage and track tasks in Asana and Slack

•  Update sales systems, lead tracking, and website content as needed

•  Data entry, spreadsheet tracking, and document management

•  Daily and weekly reporting



GRAPHICS & VISUAL CONTENT

•  Create thumbnails and social media graphics using Canva

•  Maintain consistency with brand templates and visual standards

•  Design basic promotional and content visuals as directed



WHO YOU ARE

The best candidates for this role have come from backgrounds that demanded precision — not just creativity. If your prior work required you to get things exactly right with no room for error, you'll fit here.



Backgrounds that translate well:

•  QA or compliance

•  Medical billing or insurance operations

•  Data entry auditing or claims processing

•  Paralegal or legal support

•  Finance, payroll, or bookkeeping

•  Social media management with a strong analytical component

•  Any role where following a system exactly was non-negotiable



You also need:

•  CapCut desktop — Pro/paid version — installed before your start date

•  Availability EST hours — training is 6–9 PM EST, flexible after probation

•  Strong English communication — written and verbal

•  Experience with Asana, Notion, ClickUp, or similar tools

•  Canva experience — thumbnails and basic graphics

•  Reliable internet and a working laptop capable of running CapCut desktop



NON-NEGOTIABLES

If you cannot commit to all of the following, do not apply.

•  CapCut desktop Pro installed and confirmed before start date

•  Available EST hours for training and regular check-ins

•  Follows the taught workflow exactly — no substituting your own methods

•  Takes detailed notes during training — does not ask the same question twice

•  Submits a completed QA checklist with every video

•  Does not submit work with obvious errors — you catch your own mistakes before handoff

•  Able to complete a 20-minute video edit within 4 hours once trained and independent



COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

•  $3–5 USD/hour based on experience and performance ($2.50 per hour during traning)

•  Paid weekly

•  13th-month pay

•  Monthly performance bonuses for exceeding KPIs

•  Annual retention bonus review

•  Performance-based raises

•  Promotion path — top performers can grow into team lead and management roles

•  Ongoing skill development — AI tools, social media, writing, video editing, and more

•  Stable, long-term employment with clear SOPs, systems, and feedback



HOW TO APPLY — FOLLOW EVERY STEP



Attention to detail starts with how you apply. Applications missing any step will not be reviewed.



Send an
 to 
 with the subject line:

"Applying To – Operations VA – [Your Name]"



Include ALL of the following in your
, in this exact order:

title: Real Estate Buyer Outreach Specialist for Texas SFR Portfolio
description:
Real Estate Buyer Outreach Specialist for Texas SFR Portfolio



We are hiring a Real Estate Buyer Outreach Specialist to help market and sell a Texas single-family rental portfolio.



This is not a general VA role.

This is not a general marketing role.

This is not a content creator role.

This is not just a list-building role.



We want someone who knows how to help find the right buyers, reach out the right way, qualify serious interest, follow up consistently, and help move deals forward.



About the opportunity



We are selling a portfolio of 19 single-family homes in the Texarkana, Wake Village, and Nash, Texas area, excluding one higher-priced outlier.



The portfolio includes:



8 currently leased, cash-flowing properties

5 vacant, move-in-ready properties

6 vacant properties that still need repairs or final work before move-in



Some of the vacant properties are also being leased up.



Most of the portfolio is in the $89,000 to $249,000 range.



We are open to selling:



individually

in small groups

in bundles

or as a full portfolio



Your role is to help us find and engage serious buyers and help move the process toward offers and closing 
ntum.



What we need



We are looking for someone who can:



identify relevant buyers

understand which buyer types fit which properties

write good outreach copy for different channels

use low-cost and resourceful methods first

reach out to buyers, investors, brokers, landlords, or referral partners

qualify interest

follow up consistently

track conversations and next steps

help push serious leads toward the next stage

Important note



Existing buyer access, investor databases, outreach systems, and InvestorLift experience are all strong pluses, but they are not hard requirements if you already know how to source relevant buyers quickly, use the right outreach channels, and move the process forward with minimal hand-holding.



We care more about:



resourcefulness

judgment

strong outreach copy

buyer conversations

follow-up

organization

traction

Responsibilities

source buyers, investors, brokers, landlords, and other relevant contacts

help segment buyer targets by property type

write outreach copy for different channels

do outreach through
,
, broker contact, direct messaging, or other relevant channels

handle follow-up

qualify buyer seriousness

keep pipeline organized

report clearly on activity and results

help move serious buyer conversations toward offers and next steps

Strong plus if you already

know where to find active real estate investors quickly

have experience with buyer outreach for real estate

understand investor psychology

know how to write copy that fits the platform

can operate lean without wasting budget

can show examples of similar work

can start quickly

Not a fit if

you are mainly a general VA

you only know list building but not buyer conversations

you only know content posting but not outreach

you need a lot of training to figure out where to find buyers

you do not know how to follow up and qualify leads

you cannot help move conversations toward offers

you need heavy hand-holding

To apply, send

a short intro

your experience with real estate buyer outreach or dispositions

what channels you would use first for this portfolio

whether you already have any buyer contacts, investor access, or outreach systems

2 to 3 relevant examples of similar work

sample outreach copy for at least 2 channels

what your first 3 days would look like

your availability



Please do not send a generic response.



We are looking for someone who can create real traction quickly and help move serious buyer conversations forward.



Compensation



Open to base pay plus performance bonus based on results.



Bonus may be tied to:



qualified buyer conversations

serious buyer leads

offers

and/or properties sold



We are also open to a 7-day paid trial for the right candidates.

title: Operations & Client Success Manager
description:
CLIENT EXPERIENCE & ADMIN OPERATIONS MANAGER (REMOTE)



About the Role  

We are hiring a high-performance Client Experience & Admin Operations Manager to take full ownership of our client communication, onboarding process, and backend organization.



This is NOT a basic assistant role.



This position is for someone who is organized, fast, proactive, and able to manage multiple moving parts without constant direction.



If you need to be told what to do all day, this is not for you.





Primary Objective  

Your role is to ensure every lead and client is handled professionally, quickly, and efficiently from the 
nt they enter our system.



You are responsible for keeping our business organized, responsive, and running smoothly.





Industry Context (Important)  

We operate in the tax, credit, and financial services space.



You will be communicating with clients about sensitive financial matters, so professionalism, attention to detail, and accuracy are critical.



Experience in tax preparation, credit services, or financial services is a plus, but not required.



You must be able to learn quickly and communicate in a way that builds trust with clients.





Key Responsibilities



Client Communication  

Respond to all inbound messages (DMs, texts,
s) within 5 minutes  

Maintain professional, clear, and confident communication  

Answer basic questions and direct leads appropriately  



Lead Management & Follow-Up  

Track ALL leads inside our CRM (GoHighLevel)  

Follow up with new leads, no-shows, and inactive prospects daily  

Ensure no lead goes untouched  



Appointment Setting  

Book calls and send confirmations  

Send reminders and reduce no-shows  

Keep calendar organized and updated  



Client Onboarding  

Send welcome messages and next steps  

Ensure clients submit required documents  

Track onboarding progress and follow up on incomplete items  



System & Organization  

Keep CRM updated at all times (if it’s not in the system, it doesn’t exist)  

Maintain clean and organized client records  

Identify gaps or issues in workflow and report them  



Daily Reporting  

Submit end-of-day reports including:  

Number of leads  

Conversations handled  

Appointments booked  

Follow-ups completed  





Performance Expectations (KPIs)  

Response time under 5 minutes  

100% of leads tracked in CRM  

20–30% booking rate from conversations  

Consistent daily follow-up completion  

Zero missed or ignored leads  



Requirements  

Strong communication skills (written and verbal)  

Highly organized and detail-oriented  

Able to multitask in a fast-paced environment  

Tech-savvy (CRM experience preferred, especially GoHighLevel)  

Reliable internet and consistent availability  

Proactive mindset — you solve problems, not wait for instructions  



Important  

This role directly impacts client experience, revenue, and business operations.



Mistakes, missed messages, or lack of follow-up can result in lost clients and lost income.



Accountability is non-negotiable.



Application Instructions (READ CAREFULLY)  

To be considered, you must follow all steps below:



1. Send a message with the subject line: “I’m Ready to Run Operations”  

2. Include a short paragraph explaining your experience with client communication and organization  

3. Answer this question:  

   How would you handle a lead that inquired but stopped responding?  

4. Record a short voice note (1–2 minutes) introducing yourself and explaining why you’re a good fit  



Applications that do not follow instructions will not be considered



Trial Process  

Selected candidates will complete a 3-day paid trial.



During this trial, you will be evaluated on:  

Communication speed and professionalism  

Ability to follow instructions  

Organization and attention to detail  

Follow-up consistency  



Only candidates who perform will be offered a long-term position.



We are building a structured, high-performing business.



If you are dependable, organized, and ready to take ownership, this role has strong growth potential.



If not, this is not the position for you.

title: Remote Senior Nonprofit Bookkeeper
description:
About Us

We are a USA-based accounting and advisory firm dedicated exclusively to serving nonprofit organizations and mental health practices. We manage the complete accounting cycle for a portfolio of nonprofit clients simultaneously, delivering accurate, timely, and audit-ready financial records. Our clients trust us with the financial foundation of their missions. Every tea
mber we bring on is expected to perform at a professional level from day one, follow our documented systems, and take genuine ownership of their client portfolio.



About the Role

We are seeking a highly motivated, detail-oriented Senior Bookkeeper to join our team. In this role, you will oversee comprehensive bookkeeping functions, ensuring accuracy and compliance across all financial records for a portfolio of nonprofit clients. You will work within our established systems and be held to firm-wide standards of accuracy and deadlines from day one.



This is not an entry-level role. You are expected to arrive with a fully developed competency in nonprofit accounting and the professional judgment to apply it independently across multiple clients in a remote environment.



Responsibilities

- Record and classify all transactions on an accrual basis with proper account coding, class tracking, and fund designation.

- Manage and maintain the general ledger in accordance with GAAP.

- Manage accounts payable and accounts receivable: enter vendor bills, process payments, record invoices, and apply receipts accurately.

- Prepare and post all month-end journal entries, including accruals, prepaid amortization, depreciation, and deferred revenue schedules.

- Conduct balance sheet reconciliation and review financial statements for discrepancies or errors.

- Record payroll allocation entries across cost centers, grants, and functional expense categories.

- Track restricted grants, donor contributions, and program expenses in compliance with nonprofit accounting standards.

- Produce and deliver month-end financial statements for management review.

- Support the Form 990 and audit preparation process by maintaining clean, organized records and preparing schedules as requested.

- Maintain meticulous records of journal entries, account analyses, and audit documentation to ensure audit readiness.

- Utilize accounting software, including QuickBooks Online and Xero, to manage bookkeeping operations.

- Identify opportunities to automate financial processes and increase efficiency.

- Deliver clear, accurate financial reports for internal review, client communication, and external audits.



Qualifications

 - Minimum 5 years of nonprofit accounting experience with demonstrated ability to apply accrual accounting, fund accounting, net asset classifications under ASU
, grant tracking, and functional expense allocation independently and without supervision.

 - Demonstrated ability to apply ASC 606 and ASC 842 standards, specifically managing revenue recognition based on grant documents, funder restrictions, and milestones.

- Experience managing multiple clients simultaneously in an outsourced accounting firm or agency setting.

- Advanced proficiency in QuickBooks Online (QBO), Xero, Sage, and other financial management tools.

- Advanced proficiency in project and work management tools such as Asana, Gmail, Google Sheets, Microsoft Excel, 
, Ramp, and similar platforms.

- Deep understanding of GAAP, double-entry bookkeeping, bank reconciliation, and general ledger reconciliation.

- Familiarity with governmental accounting practices and tax experience is highly desirable.

- Excellent knowledge of financial concepts, including account analysis, account reconciliation, debits and credits, journal entries, and balance sheet management.

- Ability to context-shift between multiple clients and thrive in a fast-paced, remote environment.

- Strong attention to detail and analytical skills to interpret complex financial data accurately.

- Effective written communication skills for financial reporting and client correspondence.

- High integrity, accountability, and respect for client confidentiality.

- Minimum 12 consecutive months of fully remote, self-directed work with independent deadline management.

- Problem-solving mentality with a proactive approach to process improvement and continuous learning.



Schedule and Compensation

- Part-time: 15 hours per week, with required availability between 8:00 AM and 3:00 PM EST // 9 pm and 2 am Philippines time. Hours are expected to increase to full-time as performance benchmarks are met, and the client portfolio grows

- Compensation details: $8 per hour for a trial period (1-3 months) and $10 per hour (post-trial period) 



To Apply: To be considered for this position, your application must include the following, submitted together in a single
:

 - Your resume

 - A brief message describing your experience with nonprofit fund accounting and at least one outsourced or multi-client accounting environment you have supported

- The answer to the following question, included at the top of your message: "What is the net asset classification framework introduced under ASU
, and how does it differ from the previous two-category model?"



Applications that do not follow these instructions exactly will not be reviewed.
