title: CEO Right-Hand / Property Operations VA — Assertive, Proactive, Full-Time
description:
I run a fast-moving property management business in Chicago. I need a full-time right-hand operator who can handle tenant follow-up, vendor coordination, admin tasks, scheduling, documentation, and executive support.



This is not a passive “wait for instructions” VA role. Your job is to buy back the CEO’s time, solve problems, communicate directly, and keep the business moving.







I am hiring a full-time CEO Right-Hand / Property Operations Virtual Assistant for my property management company.



We manage rental properties in Chicago, including SRO-style housing, long-term tenants, maintenance issues, rent collection, vendor coordination, paperwork, scheduling, and day-to-day operational fires.



This role is for someone who wants to become the CEO’s operational backbone.



Your job is simple:



Protect my time. Keep the business moving. Close loops.



That may include business tasks, property management tasks, tenant follow-up, vendor coordination, finance/admin work, scheduling, document organization, or executive/personal admin tasks that free up my time.



This is not a narrow job-description role where you say, “That’s not my task.”



If it is legal, professional, ethical, and it helps buy back the CEO’s time, you should be willing to help handle it.



------------------What You Will Do------------------



You will help with:

• Tenant follow-up and rent/payment coordination

• Calling or messaging tenants who are late, confused, upset, or avoiding response

• Scheduling maintenance and following up with contractors/vendors

• Tracking open issues until they are fully resolved

• Updating spreadsheets, trackers, Trello cards, Google Drive files, and internal systems

• Managing invoices, payment reminders, and admin records

• Reviewing training videos and SOPs independently

• Preparing summaries, updates, and next-step recommendations

• Helping with scheduling, travel, personal logistics, calendar coordination, and other executive-support tasks

• Taking work off my plate without needing to be spoon-fed every step



------------------Who This Role Is For------------------

This role is for you if:

• You are assertive, not timid

• You are comfortable making calls

• You can speak directly to tenants, vendors, and contractors

• You do not freeze when someone is upset

• You can self-train using videos, SOPs, and written instructions

• You like solving messy real-world problems

• You ask good questions

• You can work with a direct, demanding CEO

• You are comfortable receiving blunt feedback

• You care about getting better

• You want to become a high-trust, long-term operator inside a growing company



------------------Who Should Not Apply------------------

Do not apply if:

• You are shy on the phone

• You avoid confrontation

• You need step-by-step instructions for every task

• You only want easy admin work

• You rely on ChatGPT/AI to think for you

• You disappear when confused

• You say “done” when a task is only partially done

• You dislike direct feedback

• You are uncomfortable helping with executive/personal admin tasks

• You are looking for a low-pressure role

• You cannot work during agreed hours consistently

• You already have too many other clients or jobs



AI tools are allowed. But AI cannot replace your judgment, voice, confidence, or ownership.



------------------Required Skills------------------

You must be strong in:

• Spoken English

• Written English

• Phone communication

• Tenant/customer service

• Direct follow-up

• Google Sheets or Excel

• Gmail

• Google Drive

• Trello or similar task-management tools

• Calendar/scheduling

• Documentation

• Self-learning

• Prioritization

• Problem-solving



Property management, collections, dispatch, call center, executive assistant, legal admin, or operations experience is a major plus.



------------------Work Schedule------------------

This is a full-time role.



Expected schedule:

• 40+ hours per week

• Monday–Friday

• Must be able to work during Chicago business hours or a meaningful overlap with Chicago time.



Some weekend or urgent support may occasionally be needed, especially for tenant, maintenance, or scheduling issues.



------------------Compensation------------------



Please include your requested hourly rate in your application.



I pay for performance, reliability, ownership, and long-term value.



There will be a trial/probation period. Strong performers can earn more responsibility and better compensation over time.



------------------What Success Looks Like------------------

You are successful in this role if:

• I spend less time chasing people

• Tenants and vendors receive timely follow-up

• Open issues are tracked until closed

• Payments, invoices, and admin items do not fall through the cracks

• You proactively surface problems before they become fires

• You communicate clearly every day

• You learn the business without needing constant hand-holding

• You become someone I can trust with increasingly important work



------------------Application Instructions------------------

To apply, reply with the subject line:



RIGHT HAND OPERATOR



Then answer the following questions.

Do not send a generic cover letter. Generic applications will be ignored.





1. Direct Communication Test



A tenant is late on rent and says:



“I’m not paying the late fee. I had a hard month, and it’s unfair. Remove it.”



Write exactly what you would say back to the tenant.



Keep it short. Do not be rude. Do not be weak.





2. Judgment Test



It is Monday morning. Three things happen at the same time:



A tenant says there is no heat.

A contractor missed a scheduled repair.

A tenant is late on rent and avoiding payment.



What do you handle first, second, and third? Explain why.





3. CEO Time-Buyback Test



I tell you:



“I am traveling tomorrow. I have a meeting, a workout plan, a meal schedule, a car issue, and several business tasks open. I need you to make tomorrow easier for me.”



What steps would you take to help?



Be specific.





4. Self-Training Test



Tell me about a time you had to learn a new system, tool, or process without someone teaching you live.



What did you do?





5. Phone Confidence



Are you comfortable making outbound calls to tenants, contractors, vendors, and leads?



Answer clearly: Yes or No.



Then explain your experience.





6. Availability



What hours can you work in Chicago time?



Are you available full-time?



Do you currently have other jobs or clients?





7. Voice Sample



Record a short 60–90 second voice note answering this:



“Why would you be a strong right-hand operator for a direct, fast-moving CEO?”



Include the link in your application.

title: SPEAKING ENGAGEMENT & BRAND MANAGER (PART TIME)
description:
SPEAKING ENGAGEMENT & BRAND MANAGER

**Must have experience**

Positions Available: 1 | Part-Time 2-4hrs per day  

Location: Remote

Hourly Rate: AUD $6 – AUD $7 per hour



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a proactive, resourceful, and strategic individual to support the growth of Tristan’s personal brand and secure high-quality speaking engagements.



This role focuses on identifying, outreaching, and coordinating opportunities across podcasts, events, and platforms, while also contributing to the overall positioning and visibility of Tristan in the market.



ABOUT PURPOSE ADVISORY

We’re a heart-led, integrative professional services firm focused on helping individuals live their most fulfilled life. Our team is composed of passionate professionals across Australia and the Philippines, working together to create meaningful impact in our clients’ lives through clarity, strategy, and aligned action.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

Speaking Engagement Outreach & Booking

• Research and identify relevant podcasts, events, and speaking opportunities

• Conduct outreach to event organisers, podcast hosts, and communities

• Build and maintain a pipeline of speaking opportunities

Event Platforms Management

• Apply for relevant speaking slots and collaboration opportunities

• Create and publish event listings for Tristan-led events

• Optimise event titles, descriptions, and positioning to attract the right audience



Brand & Positioning Support

• Assist in refining Tristan’s speaking topics and messaging

• Ensure alignment between speaking opportunities and target audience

• Support content ideas that strengthen Tristan’s authority and visibility



Event & Project Coordination

• Coordinate logistics for confirmed speaking engagements

• Organise and manage Tristan-led workshops or events when required

• Track performance metrics (registrations, attendance, engagement)



CANDIDATE MUST HAVE

• Experience in marketing, branding, outreach, or business development

• Strong research skills (ability to find relevant and high-quality opportunities)

• Excellent written communication skills (clear and persuasive)

• Strong organisational and project management skills

• Ability to work independently and take initiative

• Experience using Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive)



NICE TO HAVE

• Experience booking speaking engagements or podcast outreach (A MUST!)

• Familiarity with Meetup, Humanitix, Eventbrite, or similar platforms

• Experience working with personal brands, coaches, or consultants

• Basic understanding of content strategy and audience targeting



KEY TRAITS

• Proactive and resourceful

• Strategic thinker (not just task-based)

• Detail-oriented and organised

• Strong follow-through and accountability



WE OFFER

• A flexible, remote working environment

• Opportunity to work closely with a purpose-driven brand

• Long-term growth potential within the team



APPLICATION PROCESS

To apply, please submit the following:

• A 2–3 minute video introducing yourself and your relevant experience

• Your updated CV and LinkedIn profile link

• Examples of past work related to outreach, marketing, or events (if available)



Additional Requirement:

Please include 2–3 examples of events, platforms, or communities where you think Tristan should be speaking, and explain why.



Kindly submit your complete application to 
 THOSE WITH COMPLETE APPLICATION WILL BE CONSIDERED.

title: Hospice QA Coordinator (Home Health)
description:
DO NOT APPLY if you do not meet the qualifications listed below



Position Summary



We are seeking a detail-oriented and service-driven Virtual Hospice Quality Assurance (QA)  to provide after-hours, evening, and weekend support for hospice operations.



This role is primarily responsible for quality assurance, compliance monitoring, and documentation accuracy, while also supporting intake coordination, patient call management, scheduling follow-ups, and EMR documentation.



The ideal candidate has prior hospice or home health experience, strong knowledge of documentation compliance standards, excellent English communication skills, and the ability to remain calm and professional in time-sensitive or sensitive patient situations.



Key Responsibilities

Hospice Quality Assurance (Primary Focus)



• Review and audit patient charts to ensure compliance with hospice regulations and company standards

• Track and follow up on missing, incomplete, or non-compliant documentation

• Ensure timely upload of physician orders, certifications, and required hospice documentation

• Maintain organized QA logs, audit trackers, and electronic records

• Support compliance readiness for internal reviews and external audits

• Flag documentation discrepancies and escalate issues to appropriate leadership



Intake & Documentation Support



• Assist with hospice intake processing and referral coordination

• Upload and track physician orders, certifications, and care plans in EMR

• Monitor and manage endorsement and handoff reports

• Ensure all documentation is complete, accurate, and filed timely



Scheduling & Care Coordination



• Schedule and confirm follow-up visits for hospice patients

• Coordinate with field clinicians, intake teams, and office staff

• Assist in care coordination and communication workflows

• Escalate urgent clinical or operational concerns as needed



Patient & Call Support



• Answer and triage incoming patient, family, and caregiver calls

• Provide compassionate, professional customer service aligned with hospice care standards

• Accurately document all interactions in EMR

• Route concerns promptly and appropriately



Qualifications & Requirements

Required



• Prior healthcare experience (hospice or home health strongly preferred)

• Experience with medical documentation review, QA, or compliance tracking

• Strong English communication skills (spoken and written)

• High attention to detail and strong organizational skills

• Strong computer proficiency (Microsoft Office, spreadsheets, EMR systems)

• Ability to work independently in a remote, fast-paced environment

• Ability to work after-hours, evenings, and weekends (U.S. Pacific Time)

• Reliable high-speed internet and professional remote workspace



Preferred



• Direct Hospice QA or compliance experience

• Experience with Home Health/Hospice EMR systems (e.g., Kinnser/WellSky, Axxess, Hospice MD, Homecare Homebase)

• Clinical background (RN & USRN, LVN, Medical Assistant, or allied health)

• Familiarity with hospice regulations and documentation standards



How to Apply



Please submit a short cover letter/resume highlighting your relevant experience and include your answers to the questions below:



Describe your experience in hospice or home health, including your role, duration, and responsibilities.



Do you have experience with quality assurance, chart audits, or compliance tracking? Please explain.



How many years of experience do you have in Hospice or Home Health?



What EMR systems have you used (e.g., Kinnser/WellSky, Axxess, Hospice MD, Homecare Homebase)?



Describe your experience reviewing or managing medical documentation (orders, certifications, care plans, etc.).

title: IT Manager / Field Supervisor
description:
Overview

We are seeking a seasoned IT Manager / Field Supervisor to lead our Managed Service Provider (MSP) operations with a strong focus on cloud infrastructure. This role will oversee helpdesk operations, field dispatch, and project management across a diverse client base. The ideal candidate will bring deep expertise in Azure, Microsoft 365, AWS, and hybrid environments, along with a proven ability to lead teams and deliver exceptional IT support in a fast-paced MSP setting.



Key Responsibilities

Lead and manage the MSP helpdesk, ensuring timely resolution of tickets and high client satisfaction

Coordinate and dispatch field technicians for on-site support and project implementations

Oversee cloud-centric IT projects including migrations, deployments, and infrastructure upgrades

Provide hands-on technical leadership in Azure, Microsoft 365, AWS, and hybrid environments

Mentor and manage a team of IT professionals, fostering collaboration and growth

Monitor SLAs and KPIs, and implement process improvements for operational efficiency

Collaborate with clients to assess needs, propose cloud-first solutions, and maintain strong relationships



Required Qualifications

Minimum of 10 years in IT management, with at least 5 years in an MSP helpdesk environment leading teams

Deep technical proficiency in:

Azure AD, Office 365, Intune, SharePoint, MS Exchange Server

AWS services including EC2, S3, IAM, and VPC configurations

Windows Server (on-prem and cloud-hosted)

Google Workspace and Google Cloud infrastructure

Setup and management of Ubiquiti, Cisco, SonicWall, Synology hardware

Strong project management experience in cloud migrations and IT deployments

Proven ability to manage ticket workflows and dispatch field resources effectively

Excellent leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills

Ability to work under pressure and adapt to evolving client needs



Preferred Qualifications

Certifications such as Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator, AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Cisco CCNA

Experience with MSP tools like RMM and PSA platforms

Familiarity with cybersecurity best practices and compliance standards (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR)



Why Join Us

Lead a dynamic team in a growing MSP with a cloud-first approach

Competitive salary and benefits package

Access to cutting-edge technologies and ongoing professional development

Collaborative and innovative work culture



How to Apply

Please submit your resume, a cover letter detailing your relevant experience, and a short intro video. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.



Equal Opportunity Employer

We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, or any other protected status.

title: Billing & Procurement Account Manager
description:
About Edge360 / Staff360

Edge360 / Staff360 is a trusted Managed Service Provider (MSP) and Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) agency. We help businesses streamline operations, optimize technology, and scale efficiently by providing reliable IT solutions and outsourcing support.

We’re growing fast and looking for motivated professionals who want to be part of a team that drives digital transformation.



Position Overview

We’re hiring a Billing & Procurement Account Manager who will play a key role in managing billing, procurement, and financial operations. This role requires someone detail-oriented, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast-paced environment.

You’ll be the go-to person for ensuring accuracy in billing, smooth procurement processes, and strong vendor relationships—all while supporting our MSP and BPO projects.



Key Responsibilities

Prepare accurate and competitive quotes for client projects in collaboration with sales and technical teams.

Manage procurement: sourcing, ordering, and tracking hardware, software, and other project resources.

Handle billing and invoicing, ensuring compliance with company policies and client agreements.

Maintain project plans, timelines, and budgets to support efficient delivery.

Act as the main contact for accounts payable/receivable, resolving inquiries quickly.

Work with the accounting team on reconciliations, reporting, and audits.

Manage vendor relationships, negotiate contracts, and identify cost-saving opportunities.

Assist with financial forecasting, budgeting, and process improvements.

Support ad-hoc tasks related to billing, procurement, and project management.



Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, or related field (or equivalent experience).

At least 3 years of experience in billing, procurement, or financial management—preferably in MSP, BPO, or IT services.

Proficiency in financial software (QuickBooks, SAP, etc.) and project management tools (Microsoft Project, Asana, etc.).

Strong knowledge of procurement processes, vendor management, and invoicing best practices.

Excellent communication skills and the ability to work well with both internal teams and external partners.

High attention to detail, strong analytical skills, and problem-solving ability.

Ability to multitask and prioritize in a deadline-driven environment.

Familiarity with MSP/BPO industry standards is a plus.



What We Offer

Competitive salary with performance-based bonuses

Opportunities for career growth and professional development

Flexible remote work options

A collaborative and supportive team environment

Exposure to cutting-edge projects in a growing industry



How to Apply

If this sounds like the right fit for you, send us your resume and a short intro video highlighting your relevant experience.



We look forward to welcoming the right candidate to our team!

title: AI Tools Tutor — Claude/Anthropic Ecosystem

Setting up development environment on Mac (Python, Jupyter, VS Code, terminal basics)

Walking through Anthropic Academy courseware together

Claude Code setup, configuration, and practical use

Claude Cowork setup and daily workflow integration

Claude API basics (making calls, prompt engineering, tool use)

MCP (Model Context Protocol) fundamentals



You must have:

Patience with a non-developer learner who has Bash/SQL skills but is not a programmer

title: Anthropic Claude Expert – Trainer & Integration Specialist
description:
About the Role

We are a growing London-based property maintenance company actively building AI-powered workflows into our operations. We already use Claude daily and have working integrations with 
 and ServiceM8. We are not looking for someone to introduce us to Claude — we are looking for a true expert who can take us to the next level.



This is a training, demonstration, and integration role. Sessions will be live, one-to-one or with small groups, with no preparation time between questions. You must be able to demonstrate, explain, and implement in real time.



What You Will Do

Deliver live training sessions to our team on advanced Claude usage — prompting strategies, system prompts, operator/user roles, and getting consistent, high-quality outputs

Set up and configure Claude Cowork — demonstrating how to use it for desktop automation and repetitive business tasks



Set up and configure Claude Code — showing practical use cases relevant to our workflows

Advise on and help implement integrations between Claude Cowork and ServiceM8, with a focus on automating repetitive sales lead tasks

Identify opportunities where Claude can replace or streamline manual processes across our business

Answer advanced questions in real time without needing to look things up



You Must Have

Deep, hands-on expertise with the Anthropic Claude ecosystem — not just the chat interface, but the API, system prompts, model behaviour, and tooling

Proven experience setting up and using Claude Cowork (the Anthropic desktop automation tool)

Working knowledge of Claude Code

Experience building or advising on Claude integrations with third-party platforms (ServiceM8 experience is a strong advantage)

The ability to teach confidently in live sessions — no scripts, no slides you haven't built yourself, no looking things up mid-session

Strong spoken and written English

You Will Not Be a Good Fit If:

You have only used Claude casually through the chat interface

You need preparation time to answer questions about Claude's capabilities

You are not familiar with Claude Cowork or Claude Code

You cannot demonstrate integrations with real tools in a live session

You rely on generic AI knowledge rather than deep Anthropic-specific expertise



Engagement Details

Remote role (Philippines-based candidates welcome)

Flexible hours to accommodate UK time zone (GMT/BST)

Initially session-based — potential for ongoing retainer based on performance

Rate: Negotiable based on demonstrated expertise



How to Apply

Please do not apply with a generic cover letter. To be considered, include the following:

A brief explanation of your specific experience with Claude Cowork and Claude Code



One concrete example of a Claude integration you have built or configured with a third-party platform



Your availability for a short live demonstration call — this is mandatory before any offer is made



Applications without these three elements will not be reviewed.

title: Researcher
description:
Job Title: Private Investigator / Researcher.

Type of work: Private Investigation - Contractor.



Compensation: You will begin with a compensation of $700 USD per month ($350 USD biweekly), with performance-based increases to be considered after the first, second, third, fourth and sixth months. 



As a reference point, most of our candidates are earning $850 after the sixth month. 



Schedule: Monday through Friday 9 AM - 5 PM.



About the Role



We are seeking a motivated individual to join our team as a Private Investigator (Contractor), focusing on insurance research. This is a fully remote, long-term, work-from-home position. You will be hired as an independent contractor, not as an employee.



While you will have some freedom to manage your own schedule, please note that our company operates within U.S. business hours. All completed cases must be submitted between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM (Pacific Standard Time), as support is only available during these times. Please do not apply unless you have full availability during these hours.



Please note that your performance will be reviewed at the end of the first and second months, which constitute the Onboarding/Training period. Based on the results of these evaluations, we will determine whether to continue a long-term working relationship.



Job Requirements



• Language: English (C2 - Advanced, or native proficiency). Preference will be given to candidates with a neutral or minimal accent. 

• Education: Bachelor’s degree (or higher) in any field. The specific area of study is not important, but an undergraduate degree is required.

• Experience: A minimum of 2 years of experience as an Insurance Researcher or, alternatively, at least 7 years of experience in a different field. Comprehensive in-house training will be provided to less experienced individuals.



Skills & Traits We Value



We are a hands-off management company, so success in this role requires independence and accountability. Ideal candidates will demonstrate the following:



• Strong self-motivation and discipline.

• Excellent attention to detail.

• Ability to learn quickly.

• Openness to feedback and constructive criticism.

• Effective time management skills.

• Strong problem-solving abilities.

• Personal accountability for results.

• Clear and consistent communication.



Benefits:



•       Paid training (3 Sessions). 

•       US Federal Holidays are off and paid. 

•       Somewhat Flexible.

title: Amazon sourcer
description:
ONLINE ARBITRAGE

Looking to grow our team!Seeking one part time position who will undergo a trial period of one week paid before being brought on as part time.



We are more than an Amazon arbitrage (OA) business. If you seek a long term, advancing position, this is for you.



PSA

We have systems in place to ensure we don’t bring on any bad actors. If you plan on sending us leads that are being shared across other businesses or you are pulling items off of a leads list, you will be let go immediately.



We are seeking a part time product sourcer who can grow in our company and later be promoted to a higher paid managerial role. If you are not a hard worker and can’t prove it to us over our interview process, don’t even bother completing this application.

Requirements

Reliable internet Compensation & Benefits

Unlimited PTO

Holidays Off

Bonuses which will be discussed

Communication Skills



This should be simple, we're looking for people who don't need much supervision. Someone that is dependable and eager to work. We know this is a very competitive market and we are looking for a rockstar tea
mber we can rely on for years and years to come. We need someone who pays attention to detail and understands how to properly read data. We expect the majority of the items to be shared with our team to be purchasable and pass all of our SOP checks. Loyalty is the most important thing to our team. We're looking for people who want to stay with us long term and can see themselves in management /senior roles. If you've read this far, congratulations as I've found out many don't. Please put your favorite fruit as the title of your application.

Furthermore, we will not be accepting any candidates who already work for another client as we need exclusivity. 
 will be required for team communications.

title: Paid Media Manager
description:
Overview

We are looking for a highly execution-focused Paid Media Manager to own and operate the company’s lead generation campaigns across multiple ad platforms (Meta, Google, LinkedIn). This role is built around rapid campaign deployment, continuous testing, and performance-driven iteration.



You will be responsible for launching new campaigns on a monthly cadence tied to new lead magnets, optimizing performance quickly, and scaling or killing campaigns based on results.



This is not a traditional “set it and optimize slowly” ads role. We operate in fast cycles and expect quick turnaround, clear thinking, and proactive recommendations.



Key Responsibilities

Campaign Execution

• Launch new paid campaigns within 3–5 business days of receiving assets 

• Build and manage campaigns across Meta, Google, and LinkedIn 

• Maintain a consistent campaign structure to support rapid repeat of successes and drops of failures 



Rapid Testing & Optimization

• Run structured tests on creative, copy, and audiences 

• Monitor performance daily and make adjustments quickly 

• Identify winning campaigns and scale them 

• Shut down underperforming campaigns efficiently 



Remarketing & Funnel Management

• Build and manage retargeting campaigns across platforms 

• Ensure users are brought back into the funnel for conversion 

• Optimize full-funnel performance (not just top-of-funnel clicks) 



Strategic Input

• Provide clear recommendations on: 

o Ad creative improvements 

o Messaging and hooks 

o Landing page or offer adjustments 

• Translate performance data into actionable insights 



Reporting & Visibility

• Maintain a centralized dashboard showing performance across all platforms 

• Provide simple, clear weekly performance updates 

• Ensure full transparency into campaign results and decisions 



Required Experience

• Proven experience managing paid campaigns for lead generation 

• Strong working knowledge of: 

o Meta Ads 

o Google Ads 

o LinkedIn Ads 

• Experience with retargeting and conversion-focused funnels 

• Ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously 

• Comfortable working in fast-paced, iterative environments 



What We’re Looking For

• Fast executor — you can build and launch quickly without overcomplicating 

• Data-driven — you rely on performance, not opinions 

• Proactive — you don’t wait to be told what to improve 

• Strategic thinker — you understand how ads connect to the offer and funnel 

• Comfortable with ambiguity and rapid change 



Nice to Have

• Experience with tools like Looker Studio or other reporting platforms

• Familiarity with CRM-driven audiences and lead syncing 

• Experience working with monthly campaign cycles or growth marketing teams

title: Administrative Virtual Assistant (Onboarding & Admin Support)

- This is a part-time position to start, with strong potential to move into a full-time role for the right candidate.

- Manage and organize client and internal forms using JotForm and Cognito Forms

- Support administrative processes related to tax operations

- Navigate and assist with TaxSlayer (experience preferred)

- Maintain and update CRM systems such as GoHighLevel (GHL)

- Provide general administrative support as needed

- Ensure timely responses and follow-ups via WhatsApp and other channels



Requirements:

- Proven experience as a Virtual Assistant or Administrative Assistant

Familiarity with:

- JotForm

- Cognito Forms

- GoHighLevel (GHL) (a plus)

- TaxSlayer or similar tax software (preferred but not required)

- Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

- Ability to work in a fast-paced environment

- Highly responsive and reliable

- Excellent written and verbal English communication skills

- Must be available during US business hours



Bonus Skills (Nice to Have):

- Experience handling client disputes

- Background in tax preparation or tax office support

- Experience onboarding teams or managing recruitment workflows



How to Apply:

To be considered, please submit the following:

- Your updated resume

- Your WhatsApp number (this will be our primary communication tool)



Why Join Us?

- Opportunity to grow into a full-time role

- Work with a supportive and fast-growing team

- Gain experience in the tax and financial services industry



If you’re organized, proactive, and thrive in a fast-moving environment, we’d love to hear from you!

title: Facility Monitoring & Response Specialist
description:
MISSION STATEMENT



SecureSpace Self Storage is one of the fastest-growing Self Storage platforms in the USA, with assets located primarily in high-density, urban markets. With a focus on exceptional customer service and upgraded security features, SecureSpace provides a high-quality experience that our customers can count on in any store they visit.



SCOPE OF POSITION



The Customer Service Security Support Representative at SecureSpace is responsible for maintaining facility safety and operational integrity through proactive monitoring, rapid i
 response, and exceptional customer interaction. This remote role requires a vigilant, detail-oriented individual who can assess real-time security situations, engage with law enforcement or internal teams when needed, and provide accurate documentation post-i
. With access to advanced systems such as PTI, OpenTech, Spot AI, and Turing, this tea
mber plays a crucial role in safeguarding our nationwide facilities and ensuring a seamless customer experience.



This position requires strong critical thinking, situational awareness, and communication skills, as well as a willingness to step into fast-moving situations, provide clarity, and document everything from first alert to after-action reporting.



SKILLS & EXPERIENCE:



· 2+ years in customer service, security operations, or dispatch coordination.



· High school diploma or equivalent required; college education is a plus.



· Comfortable navigating surveillance and facility management systems (PTI, OpenTech, Spot AI, Turing).



· Strong verbal communication with ability to calmly engage with law enforcement and internal teams.



· Highly organized, analytical, and able to track movement across multiple data sources.



· Self-driven, dependable, and flexible to shift between security monitoring and customer support roles.



· Experience handling confidential or sensitive information with discretion.



RESPONSIBILITIES: include (but are not limited to):



· Respond to alerts involving open gates, suspicious activity, or reported break-ins.



· Call local Police Departments (non-emergency line) when needed and guide responding officers through the site.



· Gather critical details for i
 resolution: determine if individuals involved are tenants, verify site hours, confirm if "Do Not Trespass" orders are in place, and identify how the i
 began.



· Escalate i
 to the Area Manager when necessary; follow a clear escalation path if unreachable.



· Collect and share evidence (photos, videos, system logs) with PD or internal stakeholders as needed.



· Track individuals across the site using available tools, determine current presence or departure, and provide full traceable timelines.



· Prepare and distribute After-Action Reports via
 to key stakeholders 



· Support live i
 response in real time, participate in post-event recaps, and continuously refine response protocols.



· Perform additional duties as assigned, especially in response to evolving security risks.



REPORTS TO: Security Operations Lead / Customer Success Manager



PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS:



Qualified individuals must be able to perform the position's essential duties with or without accommodation. A qualified person with a disability may request a modification or adjustment to the job or work environment to meet the physical requirements of the position. GSA/SecureSpace will attempt to satisfy requests if the accommodations needed are reasonable and no undue hardship would result.



· Ability to perform essential job functions while seated for extended periods, including working on a computer and taking phone calls, as well as sit, walk, stand, bend, squat, climb, kneel, and twist intermittently or continuously.



· Must be capable of reviewing surveillance footage, reading i
 reports, and compiling documentation with or without visual aids.



· Capability to read documents and focus on computer screens, with or without corrective lenses as may be required.



· Ability to arrange the workspace efficiently for productivity, accommodating any individual needs for ergonomic considerations.



· Willingness to transition between various tasks and responsibilities while maintaining work efficiency and effectiveness.

Experience working in a production or manufacturing environment

Strong communication and teamwork skills
